{{Function |CSWikiPage = GetDead |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns 1 if the object [[reference]] is dead. |name = GetDead |returnVal = 0, 1 |returnType = void |referenceType = [Actor] |example = 
Molerat01Ref.GetDead
This will return 1 if the Molerat is dead, and 0 otherwise. }} ==Notes== *This is more accurate than checking the actor's health because there are circumstances when the actor can die without losing all of their health (such as through [[KillActor]]). *Will not return 1 if the actor was disabled while alive, but will return 1 even if the actor was deleted with [[MarkForDelete]] or disabled after death. *Will not return 1 if an essential actor lost all it's health and was rendered unconscious. Use [[GetKnockedState]] instead to detect this state. ==See Also== *[[GetCauseofDeath]] *[[GetDeadCount]] *[[GetKnockedState]] *[[GetTimeDead]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Actor State Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns the total number of this creature/NPC type that have been killed. |name = GetDeadCount |returnVal = number |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ActorID }} |example = 
GetDeadCount CrMolerat
Returns the amount of Molerats that are dead. }} ==Notes== *On a templated actor, GetDeadCount works only on the specified reference. *Note that the parameter is an actor base ID, so you cannot use this function to test whether a named object reference has been killed. *[[Resurrect]]ing a killed actor will not lower the death count for actors of that type. This is true even of unique or referenced actors. ==See Also== *[[GetDead]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Actor State Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

Also returns -1 if the target has not taken object health damage. |name = GetDestructionStage |returnVal = damageStage |returnType = int |referenceType = ref |example = NukaBottle01Ref.GetDestructionStage }} ==See Also== *[[GetDestructionDataHealth]] *[[GetDestructionDataNumStages]] *[[OnDestructionStageChange]]

{{Function |CSWikiPage = GetDetected |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns 1 if the target [[Reference]] is currently [[:Category:Detection|detected]] by the [[calling]] [[Actor]]. In other words, checks if the calling actor detects the target reference. |name = GetDetected |returnVal = 0, 1 |referenceType = [Actor] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = TargetID |Type = reference }} |conditionFunc = Both |example = 
BuddyRef.GetDetected player
Returns 1 if Buddy can detect the player, and 0 otherwise. }} ==Notes== *This function can still return true even if the actor is [[GetDead|dead]]. This is because the [[GetDetectionValue|detection value]] does not seem to update when the actor is dead, unless the area is re-loaded. *When called from console, will print two values instead of the function result. The first is the [[GetDetectionValue|detection value]] between -100 and 100, the second is the [[GetActorLightAmount|light amount]]. *May not return the expected value if [[OnFire]] is used and the calling actor is hidden, the detection state might not be updated immediatly after the weapon is fired and the block is triggered, so it's best to use [[CallAfterFrames]] to check a few frames later so that GetDetected will return an expected value. ==See Also== *[[IsActorDetected]] *[[GetDetectionValue]] / [[GetDetectionLevel]] / [[GetPCDetectionState]] *[[GetDetectingActors]] / [[GetDetectedActors]] *[[GetWeaponDetectionSoundLevel]] / [[SetWeaponDetectionSoundLevel]] *[[CreateDetectionEvent]] *[[ForceActorDetectionValue]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Actor State Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Detection Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Used to check the current [[:Category:Detection|detection]] level for the calling actor for the TargetActor. |name = GetDetectionLevel |returnVal = 0, 1, 2, 3 |returnType = int |referenceType = [Actor] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = target |Type = actorRef }} |conditionFunc = Both |example = 
Molerat01Ref.GetDetectionLevel player
Returns a value based upon how well the molerat detects the player. }} ==Notes== In testing this function appears to be broken as it does not return any value other than 0 when the player is the target. It will only return a value of 3 or 0 for all other cases where the player is not the target. * Returns 0-3 for the levels of detection: ** 0 = Lost ("Undetected") ** 1 = Unseen ("Caution") ** 2 = Noticed ("Danger") ** 3 = Seen (flashing "Danger") ==See Also== *[[GetDetectionValue]] *[[GetDetected]] *[[IsActorDetected]] *[[GetPCDetectionState]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Detection Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns what emotion an actor is using in dialogue. |name = GetDialogueEmotion |returnVal = emotion |returnType = int |example =

 elseif BuddyRef.GetDialogueEmotion == 4

{{Function |CSWikiPage = GetEquipped |origin = GECK1 |summary = Checks whether or not the calling actor has equipped the target object. |name = GetEquipped |returnType = bool |referenceType = [Actor] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Target |Type = ObjectID }} |example =

Evaluates True (1) if the player is wearing Lincoln's Hat }} ==Notes== * This function will only check for Armor and Weapon objects. It will not detect Armor Addon objects, such as integrated gloves or pieces of weapons like the Power Fist. * The argument can also be a [[Form List]], in which case the function returns true if any item in the list is currently equipped by the PC. ==See Also== *[[IsEquipped]] *[[EquipItem]] *[[GetEquippedCurrentHealth]] *[[GetEquippedEx]] *[[GetEquippedObject]] (returns the actual object at a given slot) *[[UnequipItem]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Object_Functions]] [[Category:Condition_Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns the combat reaction type between two factions. |name = GetFactionCombatReaction |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = faction |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = faction |Type = form }} }} Return values: *0 = Neutral: This is how all factions relate to each other by default, even if you don't specify it. Neutrals will be attacked by Very Aggressive actors. *1 = Enemy: Enemy actors are attacked on sight by Aggressive and Very Aggressive actors. *2 = Ally: Allies cannot attack each other under any circumstances. An actor's Assistance setting determine whether it will help an Ally in combat or not. *3 = Friend: Friends cannot attack each other under any circumstances. An actor's Assistance setting determine whether it will help a Friend in combat or not. ==See Also== *[[GetFactionReaction]] *[[SetAlly]] *[[SetEnemy]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Faction Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]] [[Category:Condition Functions]]

If the actor is not in the faction, will return value as -1. |name = GetFactionRank |returnVal = factionRank |returnType = int |referenceType = [actor:ref] |example = player.GetFactionRank BrotherhoodMq11Faction }} ==Bugs== * The console version of this command is broken and always returns -1.

[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Faction Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns the number of friendly hits made by the target actor on the calling actor. |name = GetFriendHit |returnVal = hits |returnType = int |referenceType = [Actor] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Target |Type = ActorRef }} |example = 
BuddyRef.GetFriendHit player
Returns the number of times the player has hit Buddy. }} ==Notes== *This is usually used in dialogue conditions, to check whether or not the player has attacked a 'friend,' and making the actor say an appropriate warning. ==See Also== *[[GetAttacked]] *[[GetHitAttacker]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Condition_Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns the value of a global variable. |name = GetGlobalValue |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = GlobalVar |Type = Form }} }} ==Notes== *This is usually only used in dialog conditions and the like, because in scripts you can simply look up the value of a global by using:
set fSomeFloat to GlobalVarName
In scripts, GetGlobalValue will also accept the varname string as an argument, with or without quotation marks, but this is really just you directly typing in the FormID. GetGlobalValue won't accept string variables, only ref variables.
==See Also== *[[GetQuestVariable]] *[[GetScriptVariable]] *[[ShowQuestVars]] *[[GetGlobalVariable]]

A return value of 1 means the actor is in combat but fighting by themselves. |name = GetGroupMemberCount |returnVal = combatGroupCount |returnType = int |referenceType = [actor:ref] }} ==See Also== *[[GetGroupTargetCount]]

It will return 0 if the reference is not an actor, the actor is not in combat, or if the actor is in combat but has no targets. |name = GetGroupTargetCount |returnVal = combatGroupCount |returnType = int |referenceType = [actor:ref] }} ==See Also== *[[GetGroupMemberCount]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns whether the player has the specified note parameter in their pipboy. It is not called on a Reference and it takes one parameter, the note to test for. Returns 0 if the note is not there and 1 if it is there. |name = GetHasNote |returnVal = hasNote |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = note |Type = baseform }} |example = GetHasNote BombNote Returns 0 if the bomb note is not there and 1 if it is there. }} ==See Also== *[[AddNote]] *[[GetItemCount]] *[[RemoveNote]] <!-- =='''Reference'''== None provided. [[GetHasNote_Reference|List of functions that use GetHasNote]] (wiki) --> [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Player Functions]] [[Category:Note Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns the angle between the [[calling reference]] and the specified object in a range from -180 to 180 degrees. If the calling reference is not an actor, will just return 0. |name = GetHeadingAngle |returnVal = -180 to 180 |returnType = float |referenceType = [Object] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ObjectRef |Type = ref }} |example = Player.GetHeadingAngle StrangeStatue }} ==Notes== *Do not confuse this function with GetAngle Z, which returns the heading angle of an object relative to the world. *The direction the calling reference is facing is considered 0 degrees. *Angles measured clockwise from 0 are positive, while angles measured counter-clockwise from 0 are negative. *In the example above, if the player is facing north and the object StrangeStatue is due west of the player, the returned value would be -90. *When you add this value to the calling references's current heading angle (Z angle), they will face the specified ObjectRef:
set angle to Player.GetAngle Z + Player.GetHeadingAngle StrangeStatue Player.SetAngle Z angle
==See Also== *[[GetAngle]] *[[SetAngle]] / [[SetAngleEx]] *[[Rotate]] *[[GetPos]] *[[SetPos]] / [[SetPosEx]] *[[GetHeadingAngleAlt]] / [[GetHeadingAngleBetweenPoints]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category: Functions]] [[Category: Condition Functions]] [[Category: Movement Functions]] [[Category:Movement Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Determines how injured an [[actor]] is. Health percentage is measured as a value from 0 to 1.0. |name = GetHealthPercentage |returnVal = healthPercent |returnType = float |example = If JohnDoeRef.GetHealthPercentage < 0.50 Checks to see if John Doe is below 50% of his base health. }} ==Notes== *Base effects modifying Health actor value such as IncreaseHealth will make the function return values higher than 1. *The code that is executed by this function is equivalent to: 
float fHealthPerc
int iBaseHealth 

iBaseHealth = actor.GetBaseActorValue Health
if (iBaseHealth)
   fHealthPerc = (actor.GetActorValue Health) / iBaseHealth 
else
   fHealthPerc = 1.0
endif
==See Also== *[[GetBaseHealth]] *[[GetCurrentHealth]] *[[ResetHealth]] *[[SetCurrentHealth]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Actor Value Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns the hit location of the most recent hit. |name = GetHitLocation |returnVal = LocationIndex |returnType = int |referenceType = [Actor] |example = if BuddyRef.GetHitLocation == 0 This would determine if Buddy got hit in the torso. }}
==Notes== *This information is not available in the [[OnHit]] and [[OnHitWith]] script blocks (fixed in JIP NVSE Plugin v55.60). *This information is available in a [[UDF]] event handler script for [[OnHit]] and [[OnHitWith]] *This information is available in a [[UDF]] script for [[SetOnCriticalHitEventHandler]] *GetHitLocation won't work if used in a weapon's [[Object Effect]], but will work if used in the weapon's Crit Effect. *GetHitLocation will only return the correct value in a [[GameMode]] block in the frame that the reference is killed. *The body locations and corresponding numbers are:
{| {{Wikitable}} ! Body Part !! Return Value |- | None | -1 |- | Torso | 0 |- | Head 1 | 1 |- | Head 2 | 2 |- | Left Arm 1 | 3 |- | Left Arm 2 | 4 |- | Right Arm 1 | 5 |- | Right Arm 2 | 6 |- | Left Leg 1 | 7 |- | Left Leg 2 | 8 |- | Left Leg 3 | 9 |- | Right Leg 1 | 10 |- | Right Leg 2 | 11 |- | Right Leg 3 | 12 |- | Brain | 13 |- | Hand or Weapon | 14 |} *Always returns -1 for hits that originated from an [[Explosion]].
==Human Body Parts== The body parts of a typical human actor are '''0''' (Torso), '''1''' (Head 1), '''3''' (Left Arm 1), '''5''' (Right Arm 1), '''7''' (Left Leg 1), and '''10''' (Right Leg 1). This information is defined in the ''DefaultBodyPartData'' [[BodyPartData]] object.
==See Also== *[[GetCauseofDeath]] *[[GetHitLocationLingering]] *[[IsLimbGone]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

revision.comment: replaced old beth wiki link to uesp because beth STILL has not brought theirs back

revision.text:
{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns 1 if the actor is currently ignoring crime. |name = GetIgnoreCrime |returnVal = ignoringCrime |returnType = int }} ==Notes== *The values for the flag mean: 
0	Report crimes the player commits (default).
1	Do not report crimes the player commits.
==See Also== *[[IgnoreCrime]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Crime Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]] [[Category:Condition Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns true if the actor's current cell includes the argument as the first part of its cell name. |name = GetInCell |returnVal = 0, 1 |returnType = int |referenceType = [Actor] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Type = CellID }} |example = 
Player.GetInCell Megaton
Returns a 1 if the player is in a cell that begins with "Megaton" and 0 otherwise. So this would return a 1 if the player was in MegatonPlayerHouse, MegatonCratersideSupply, etc... }} ==Notes== *The CellName has to be a valid interior cell name. Exterior cells will not work. In some cases, it is necessary to create a "dummy" interior cell (e.g. Megaton) in order to use GetInCell for a set of related interior and exterior cells (e.g. all the MegatonXXX cells). ==See Also== *[[GetInSameCell]] *[[GetInWorldspace]] *[[GetInGrid]] (Useful for exteriors) *[[GetInGridInCell]] *[[GetParentCell]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Actor State Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Cell Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns 1 if the calling actor is a member of the specified faction. |name = GetInFaction |returnVal = inFaction |returnType = int |referenceType = [actor:ref] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = faction |Type = baseform }} |example = GetInFaction MegatonFaction }} ==Notes== * GetInFaction overrides "Allow Default Dialogue" flag restrictions when used as a condition on dialogue.
==See Also== *[[AddToFaction]] *[[GetInFactionList]] *[[GetFactionRank]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Faction Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns true if the [[calling reference]] is in the same cell as the target Actor. |name = GetInSameCell |returnVal = |returnType = bool |referenceType = Actor |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Target |Type = Actor }} |example =
BuddyRef.GetInSameCell player
This will return a 1 if Buddy is in the same cell as the player, and 0 otherwise. }} ==Notes== *This function may give unexpected results if used in exteriors, as it will return false if two adjacent actors are seperated by a cell boundary. Consider using [[GetInGrid]] instead. *This function accepts NPC/creature base forms but you should NOT use these as it will cause your script to fail. ==See Also== *[[GetInCell]] *[[GetInGrid]] *[[IsInInterior]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Actor State Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Cell Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns 1 if the [[calling]] [[Actor]] is currently in one of the exterior [[Cell]]s of the specified [[Worldspace]]. Always returns 0 if the actor is in an interior cell. |name = GetInWorldspace |returnVal = 0, 1 |returnType = int |referenceType = [Actor] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = worldspace |Type = ref }} |example = 
BuddyRef.GetInWorldspace WastelandNV
}} ==See Also== *[[IsInInterior]] *[[GetInCell]] *[[GetParentWorldspace]] *[[GetParentCell]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Actor_State_Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns a 1 if the designated target is in the [[Encounter Zone]]. |name = GetInZone |returnVal = inZone |returnType = int |referenceType = ref |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = zone |Type = baseform }} |example = FredRef.GetInZone ParadiseFallsZone Returns 1 if FredRef is currently in a cell attached to the ParadiseFallsZone }} =='''Reference'''== None provided. <!-- [[IsChild_Reference|List of functions that use IsChild]] (wiki) -->
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]] [[Category:Encounter_Zone_Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns 1 if the [[Actor]] is in an alerted state. This is purely an animation state, and does not affect their actual combat status. |name = GetIsAlerted |returnVal = 0, 1 |returnType = int |referenceType = [ActorRefID] |example = if BuddyRef.GetIsAlerted }} ==See Also== *[[SetAlert]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Actor State Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:AI Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Tests if a selected actor has a specified alignment (Good, Evil, Neutral). The function returns a 1 if the alignment matches and returns 0 if it does not. |name = GetIsAlignment |returnVal = isAlignment |returnType = int |referenceType = [actor:ref] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = alignment |Type = string }} |example = FlashRef.GetIsAlignment Neutral Returns a 1, because Flash has a neutral alignment. }} ==Alignment Values== * VeryGood * Good * Neutral * Evil * VeryEvil
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Karma Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns 1 if the actor's class matches a given Class ID. |name = GetIsClass |returnType = boolean |referenceType = actor |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Class |Type = form }} |example = if SomeActor.GetIsClass Doctor ; Do my doctor stuff endif }}
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Actor State Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns 1 if the actor is a [[:Category:Creatures|creature]] ([[Form_Type_IDs|form type]] 43). |name = GetIsCreature |returnType = bool |referenceType = [actor:ref] }} ==See Also== *[[GetCreatureType]] *[[GetIsCreatureType]] *[[IsActorAlt]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Actor State Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Creature Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns 1 if [[creature]] is of the creature type specified (in parameter). |name = GetIsCreatureType |returnType = bool |referenceType = [actor:ref] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = creatureType |Type = int }} |example = If BuddyRef.GetIsCreatureType 5 == 1 This would determine if Buddy is of creature type #5 (a feral ghoul). }} {{Creature_Types}}
==See Also== *[[GetCreatureType]] *[[GetIsCreature]]
<!-- =='''Reference'''== None provided. [[GetIsCreatureType_Reference|List of functions that use GetIsCreatureType]] (wiki) -->
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Creature Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns 1 if the object is the specified [[Form_Type_IDs|form type]]. |name = GetIsFormType |returnType = bool |referenceType = ref |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = pFormType |Type = string }} |example = GetIsFormType ACTI Returns 1 if the object is an Activator }} ==Notes== * pFormType is the 4 character form type string (ACTI, CONT, CREA, etc.) which is displayed in the ALL section of the object window.
==See Also== *[[GetIsID]] *[[GetIsObjectType]] *[[GetObjectType]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns 1 if the [[actor]] [[reference]] is in the "ghost" state. Its effects are detailed on [[SetGhost]]'s page. |name = GetIsGhost |returnVal = 0, 1 |returnType = int |referenceType = [Actor] |example = 
BuddyRef.GetIsGhost
Returns a 1 if Buddy is set as a ghost, and 0 otherwise }} ==See Also== *[[SetGhost]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Actor_State_Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]] [[Category:Condition Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns 1 if the [[calling reference]] is a reference to the specified [[EditorID]] of a [[Base Object]]. |name = GetIsID |returnVal = 0, 1 |returnType = bool |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ObjectID |Type = base }} |example = Begin Onequip Player ; One script for a whole set of armor if GetIsID myPowerArmor == 1 unequip myPowerArmor endif end }}
==Notes== * Cannot be used to check the player's EditorID, instead use the player FormID like so: MyREF.GetIsID 7 == 1.
==See Also== *[[EditorIDToFormID]] *[[GetEditorID]] *[[GetIsFormType]] *[[GetSelf]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Actor State Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:ID Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns 1 if the lock on the [[calling reference]] has been broken, 0 otherwise.
If the player has the "Ignore Broken Locks" [[Perk Entry Point]] set to a non-zero value and they have broken the lock for the first time, they still have a second chance to lockpick, hence ''GetIsLockBroken'' will return 0. |name = GetIsLockBroken |returnType = bool |referenceType = reference }} ==See Also== *[[GetLocked]] *[[GetLockLevel]] *[[Lock]] *[[Unlock]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Lock Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns 1 if the object is the specified [[Form_Type_IDs|form type]]. |name = GetIsObjectType |returnType = bool |referenceType = ref |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = formType |Type = string }} |example = GetIsObjectType Activator Returns 1 if the object is an Activator }} ==Notes== * FormType is the full form type string ([[Activator]], [[Container]], [[Creature]], etc.) which is displayed in the ALL section of the object window.
=='''See Also'''== *[[GetIsFormType]] *[[GetObjectType]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]] [[Category:Condition Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns if the actor is a playable in-game race. For example, races such as Ghouls, and specifically made "Raider races" will return a 0 value as not being playable, while the "normal races" will return a 1. Does not take a base form parameter; it only checks if the reference npc's race is playable. |name = GetIsPlayableRace |returnType = bool |referenceType = [npc:ref] }} ==See Also== *[[IsPlayable]] *[[LNIsPlayable]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]] [[Category:Condition Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns True (1) if the actor's race matches a given RaceId. |name = GetIsRace |returnType = boolean |referenceType = actor |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Race |Type = form }} |example = if SomeActor.GetIsRace Ghoul ; Do my ghoulish stuff endif }} ==See Also== *[[GetRace]] (to return the race id itself)
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Actor State Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns 1 if the calling object is the specified reference. As opposed to [[GetIsID]], which checks against the base object ID as defined in the object window, this function takes a specific reference as a parameter. The reference must be persistent. |name = GetIsReference |returnVal = 0, 1 |returnType = bool |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = reference |Type = ref }} |example = 
  if GetIsReference NPC1 == 1
   NPC1.moveto player
  elseif GetIsReference NPC2 == 1
   NPC1.moveto player
  endif
}} [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns 1 if the calling actor is of the specified sex (male/female). |name = SetGlobalValue |returnType = bool |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = sex |Type = string }} }} ==Examples== 
If GetIsSex Male == 1

If Player.GetIsSex Female
==See Also== *[[GetPCIsSex]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Actor State Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Checks if an actor is using a specified item, returning 1 if they match. |name = GetIsUsedItem |returnType = boolean |referenceType = actor |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = item |Type = baseform }} |example = GetIsUsedItem NukaCola Returns a 1 if the actor is using a NukaCola. }} ==See Also== *[[GetIsUsedItemType]] *[[GetIsUsedItemEquipType]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Checks if an item that matches an [[Equip Type]] is being used or activated, returning 1 if they do. |name = GetIsUsedItemEquipType |returnVal = TypeMatches |returnType = bool |referenceType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = EquipType |Type = int }} |example = GetIsUsedItemEquipType Food Returns a 1 if the actor is using a Food object. }} ==See Also== *[[GetIsUsedItemType]] *[[GetIsUsedItem]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns 1 if the calling actor has the specified voice type. |name = GetIsVoiceType |referenceType = [actor:ref] |returnType = bool |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = voiceType |Type = baseform }} }}
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns the amount of ObjectID in the container\actor\creature. |name = GetItemCount |referenceType = reference |returnVal = count |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = item |Type = form }} |example = 
Player.GetItemCount Caps001
==Example== 
 If Player.GetItemCount Caps001 == 0
   Player.AddItem Caps001 100
 EndIf
}} ==Notes== *You can use a reference variable as ObjectID. *You can use a [[FormList]] as the item parameter -- in this case, the function returns the sum of the counts for all items in the list. *Does not work with [[Notes]]. Use [[GetHasNote]] to check if the Player has a specific note. ==Bugs== *Sometimes returns a non 0 value for items that are not in fact in the container because of items added via leveled lists. Fixed in JIP LN 55.41. ==See Also== *[[AddItem]] *[[AddItemAlt]] *[[BaseGetItemCount]] *[[GetContainerInventoryCount]] *[[GetRefCount]] - for stacks of an item reference. *[[RemoveItem]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Object Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns actor's current knocked state. |name = GetKnockedState |referenceType = [actor:ref] |returnType = int }} ==Knocked States== 
-1	fatigue knockout
0	normal 
1	knocked out (includes unconscious essential actor, paralyzed actor, and negative fatigue knockout.)
2	knocked down
==Notes== * This function will return 0 if called on actors knocked down by [[PushActorAway]] command. * If an essential actor is knocked out before recovering from [[PushActorAway]] then GetKnockedState will act as though they are still being affected by [[PushActorAway]] and will return 0. [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]] [[Category:Condition Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Indicates whether the last time an actor was hit resulted in a critical strike. The information is available in the ON HIT script block. |name = GetLastHitCritical |returnType = bool |example = if GetLastHitCritical Condition is true if last hit was a critical. }} ==Notes== * Information is available in the [[OnHit]] and the [[OnHitWith]] block types. * This function is available in the Condition Item menu, but it is unclear if the function will behave correctly if used in this instance.
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Used to get an actor's current level. |name = GetLevel |returnVal = level |returnType = int |referenceType = [actor:ref] |example = Player.Getlevel Will return the player's current level. }} ==See Also== * [[SetLevel]] <!-- =='''Reference'''== --> [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = '''GetLinkedRef ''' gives the reference that is currently linked to the current reference. For example, if a switch opens a door, you can link the switch to the door and if the switch is activated you get the door reference and activate it. It is called on a reference and takes no parameters. |name = GetLinkedRef |referenceType = reference |returnType = reference |example = 
scn SwitchScript

ref MyMarker 

Begin OnActivate

Set MyMarker to GetLinkedRef

    if MyMarker.GetDisabled == 0 ;if marker is enabled...
         PlaySound OBJSwitchButtonB
         MyMarker.Disable

else
         PlaySound OBJSwitchButtonA
         MyMarker.Enable

endif
end
A simple, completed example of a switch script that uses '''GetLinkedRef'''. The [[activator]] that will hold this script should have an [[Reference#Enable_Parent|enable parent]] added to it as its [[Reference#Linked_Ref|linked ref]]. }} =='''See Also'''== *[[SetLinkedReference]] *[[Reference]] *[[GetDisabled]] *[[Disable]] *[[Enable]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Reference Variable Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns the lock level of a [[Terminal]] or a Lock ([[Door]]s or [[Container]]s). |name = GetLockLevel |returnType = int |referenceType = ref |example = if TerminalRef01.GetLockLevel < 5 ==Example== 
if ContainerRef01.GetLockLevel < 25
}} ==Lock Levels== *Return values for a lock (door or container): 
0	very easy 
25	easy 
50	average 
75	hard 
100	very hard 
255	impossible
*Return values for a terminal: 
0	very easy 
1	easy 
2	average 
3	hard 
4	very hard 
5	impossible
==Notes== *Lock level represents lockpicking/hacking difficulty only and should not be used to determine access state. [[GetLocked]] can be used to determine that. *If the terminal is leveled, the value you get will be the leveled value. (same as locks) ==See Also== *[[GetLocked]] *[[Lock]] *[[Unlock]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Lock_Functions]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = The '''GetLocked''' script function gets the access state of [[Terminal|terminals]] and locks ([[Door]]s or [[Container]]s). Note that in vanilla ''Fallout 3'' and ''New Vegas'', hacking a terminal does not change the access state as far as GetLocked is concerned, and unless unlocked via script, GetLocked will return 1 for any terminal that is or was previously locked (fixed by JIP LN version 54.80 in New Vegas and in [[GetLockedAlt]] from [[ButcherPete FOSE]] in Fallout 3). |name = GetLocked |returnType = int |referenceType = ref |example = if DoorRef01.GetLocked == 1 Returns whether or not a door is locked but not locked out. }} ==Return Values== *Doors/Containers: 
0	unlocked (player has used a key or door is not locked)
1	locked but can be opened with key or lockpick
2	locked out (lock level is impossible or lock is broken)
*Terminals: 
0	unlocked (player has note, terminal has no lock data, or terminal unlocked through script). Does not change state when hacked(¹).
1	has lock data. Does not change stage when hacked.
2	locked out (player failed at hacking or lock level is impossible)
*(¹)As of version 54.80, JIP LN repairs this function so that GetLocked will accurately return the access state of a terminal, as opposed to whether or not it has lock data. ==See Also== *[[GetLockLevel]] *[[GetLockedOut]] *[[Lock]] *[[Unlock]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Lock_Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns the total number of major crimes the player has committed against the specified NPC. |name = GetMajorCrimeCount |returnVal = crimeCount |returnType = int |referenceType = [actor:ref] |example = FlashREF.GetMajorCrimeCount }} ==See Also== *[[ClearActorsFactionsPlayerEnemyFlag]] *[[ClearFactionPlayerEnemyFlag]] *[[GetActorFactionPlayerEnemy]] *[[GetActorCrimePlayerEnemy]] *[[GetMinorCrimeCount]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Crime Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns the visible state of the calling [[map marker]] [[reference]]. |name = GetMapMarkerVisible |returnVal = markerState |returnType = int |referenceType = markerRef |example = set iMarkerState to rMarker.GetMapMarkerVisible To check for visibility, regardless of ability to travel: if (rMarker.GetMapMarkerVisible) 	;marker is visible, but may not be fast travel enabled. endif }}
==Marker State== 0	Marker is not visible on the map. 1	Marker is visible on the map, but not fast travel enabled. 2	Marker is visible ''and'' fast travel enabled.
==See Also== *[[ShowMap]] *[[ShowAllMapMarkers]] *[[Reference#Map_Marker|Map Marker Data]] *[[SetMapMarkerVisible]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Map Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns the total number of minor crimes the player has committed against the specified NPC. |name = GetMinorCrimeCount |returnVal = crimeCount |returnType = int |referenceType = [actor:ref] |example = FlashREF.GetMinorCrimeCount }} ==See Also== *[[ClearActorsFactionsPlayerEnemyFlag]] *[[ClearFactionPlayerEnemyFlag]] *[[GetActorFactionPlayerEnemy]] *[[GetActorCrimePlayerEnemy]] *[[GetMajorCrimeCount]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Crime Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns 1 if the specified objective is marked as completed, 0 otherwise. |name = GetObjectiveCompleted |returnVal = completed |returnType = bool |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = quest |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = objectiveIndex |Type = int }} |example = GetObjectiveCompleted MyQuest 10 See if objective 10 is completed. }} ==See Also== *[[GetQuestCompleted]] *[[GetQuestCompletedAlt]] *[[SetObjectiveCompleted]]
<!-- [[GetObjectiveCompleted_Reference|List of functions that use GetObjectiveCompleted]] (wiki) --> [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Quest Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns 1 if the specified objective is currently being displayed, 0 otherwise. |name = GetObjectiveDisplayed |returnVal = completed |returnType = bool |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = quest |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = objectiveIndex |Type = int }} |example = GetObjectiveDisplayed MyQuest 10 See if objective 10 is being displayed. }} ==Notes== * If the objective is displayed in Pip-Boy, the function will return 1 regardless of whether the objective or the quest it belongs in has been marked as completed. If one wishes to only check against active objectives, an additional check using [[GetObjectiveCompleted]] is required.
==See Also== *[[SetObjectiveDisplayed]] <!-- [[GetObjectiveDisplayed_Reference|List of functions that use GetObjectiveDisplayed]] (wiki) --> [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Quest Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns true if the calling actor offers services in his current package. |name = GetOffersServicesNow |referenceType = actorRef |returnType = bool }} ==Notes== * This command will return false if the npc gets into a conversation with another npc or while moving to a destination. * If you set up a travel package (even with the "offer service" flag on) while the NPC is traveling to its destination it is in a temporary NOT OFFER SERVICE state while they are moving. * Using the function as a condition in vendor dialogue can cause crashes in the ''New Vegas'' engine when another vendor is setup through a talking activator such as The Sink.
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Gets the open state of an animating door |name = GetOpenState |returnVal = 0, 1, 2, 3, 4 |returnType = int |referenceType = [Door] |example = 
ShackDoor.GetOpenState
Will return the state of the shack door }} ==Notes== *GetOpenState will return relevant values for some objects which have Open and Closed animation states, including Radios and some Containers. *The following correspond to the state of the door: 
    0 = none (has no open/closed states) 
    1 = open 
    2 = opening 
    3 = closed 
    4 = closing
*Note that these states do NOT correspond to those of [[SetOpenState]] ==See Also== *[[SetOpenState]] *[[GetDefaultOpen]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns whether the player is an enemy of the specified faction or not. |name = GetPCEnemyOfFaction |returnType = bool |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = faction |Type = baseform }} |example = if GetPCEnemyOfFaction AntFaction ;do stuff because the ants hate the player }} ==See Also== *[[SetPCEnemyOfFaction]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Faction Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns 1 if the player is currently expelled from the specified faction. |name = GetPCExpelled |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = FactionID |Type = editorID }} |example = set bExpelled to GetPCExpelled MagesGuild }} ==See Also== *[[SetPCExpelled]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Faction Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns 1 if Player is a member of the specified Faction. |returnVal = 0, 1 |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Faction |Type = ref }} |example = set isInFaction to GetPCInFaction VCaesarsLegionFaction }} ==See Also== *[[GetFactionRank]] *[[GetInFaction]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Faction Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Same as [[GetIsRace]], but only applies to the player. Mainly useful as a condition function. |name = GetPCIsRace |returnVal = 0, 1 |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Race |Type = RaceID }} |example = 
GetPCIsRace Asian
Returns 1 (true) if the player is Asian }} ==See Also== *[[GetIsRace]] *[[SameRace]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Player_Functions]] [[Category:Condition_Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns the player's gender. |name = GetPCIsSex |returnVal = 0, 1 |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Sex |Value = Male, Female }} |example = 
If GetPCIsSex Male
     ;execute code if the player is male
endif

If GetPCIsSex Female
     ;execute code if the player is female
endif
This will determine the gender of the player, and run code accordingly }} ==See Also== *[[GetIsSex]] *[[SameSex]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Player_Functions]] [[Category:Condition_Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns the specified player misc statistic. |name = GetPCMiscStat |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = miscStat |Type = string }} |example = GetPCMiscStat "Locks Picked" Returns number of locks the player has picked }} ==Available Statistics==
===Fallout 3===
*"Bobbleheads Found" *"Books Read" *"Captives Rescued" *"Chems Taken" *"Computers Hacked" *"Contracts Completed" *"Corpses Eaten" *"Creatures Killed" *"Locations Discovered" *"Locks Picked" *"Mines Disarmed" *"Mysterious Stranger Visits" *"Pants Exploded" *"Paralyzing Punches" *"People Killed" *"People Mezzed" *"Pockets Picked" *"Quests Completed" *"Rad-X Taken" *"RadAway Taken" *"Robots Disabled" *"Sandman Kills" *"Speech Successes" *"Stimpaks Taken" *"Times Addicted" *"Weapons Created"
===Fallout: New Vegas===
*"Barter Amount Traded" *"Blackjack Games Played" *"Books Read" *"Caravan Games Lost" *"Caravan Games Won" *"Challenges Completed" *"Chems Taken" *"Computers Hacked" *"Corpses Eaten" *"Creatures Killed" *"Disintegrations" *"Dismembered Limbs" *"Doctor Bags Used" *"Have Limbs Crippled" *"Health From Food" *"Health From Stimpaks" *"Items Crafted" *"Items Repaired" *"Locations Discovered" *"Locks Picked" *"Mines Disarmed" *"Miss Fortunate Occurrences" *"Mysterious Stranger Visits" *"Pants Exploded" *"Paralyzing Punches" *"People Killed" *"Pockets Picked" *"Quests Completed" *"Rad-X Taken" *"RadAway Taken" *"Robots Disabled" *"Roulette Games Played" *"Sandman Kills" *"Slots Games Played" *"Speech Failures" *"Speech Successes" *"Stimpaks Taken" *"Times Addicted" *"Times Slept" *"Total Things Killed" *"Water Consumed" *"Weapon Modifications" *"Weapons Created"
==See Also== *[[ModPCMiscStat]] *[[ModExtraMiscStat]] / [[GetExtraMiscStat]] *[[Stats List]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category: Functions]] [[Category: Player Functions]] [[Category: Condition Functions]] [[Category:Engine Differences (Fallout)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]] [[Category: Stat Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns the number of hours the player has set to sleep from the menu. Returns 0 if the player is not sleeping. |name = GetPCSleepHours |returnType = int |example = 
GetPCSleepHours
Will return how many hours the player has set to sleep. }} ==See Also== *[[SetPCSleepHours]] *[[ShowSleepWaitMenu]] *[[WakeUpPC]] *[[IsTimePassing]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Player_Functions]] [[Category:Time Functions]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns the calling [[actor]]'s current [[package]] target (if any). |name = GetPackageTarget |returnVal = Target |returnType = ref |referenceType = [Actor] |example = 
BuddyRef.GetPackageTarget
Returns the reference of Buddy's current AI package target }} ==Notes== *If GetPackageTarget is called on an actor in a conversation, it returns the reference of the actor's conversation partner. [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Reference Variable Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:AI Functions]] [[Category:Package Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns the enable parent reference, as set in the Enable Parent tab of the [[Reference]] dialog. A reference with an enable parent will disable/enable in accordance with its parent |name = GetParentRef |returnType = ref |referenceType = [Actor, Activator] |example = 
ref myParent
set myParent to BuddyRef.GetParentRef
This will set ''myParent'' to BuddyRef's enable parent }} ==See Also== *[[SetEnableParent]] *[[GetLinkedRef]] *[[GetDisabled]] *[[Disable]] *[[Enable]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Reference Variable Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

revision.comment: Specify how this works with certain perks
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns the permanent [[Stats_List|actor value]] with no bonus from situational perks, equipped items, drugs, etc.
Behaves like [[GetBaseActorValue]], except [[items]] (e.g. skill books) that add permanent Actor Value modifiers are included. *While the effects of the '''Tag!''' [[Perk]] are accounted for, that is solely because it uses the [[AddTagSkills]] function to permanently modify the chosen AV. Other perks that use [[Perk Entry Point]]s to modify the effective value of an AV are NOT accounted for. |name = GetPermanentActorValue |alias = GetPermAV |returnType = float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = statName |Type = string }} |example = JohnRef.GetPermanentActorValue Barter }} ==See Also== *[[Stats List]] *[[ForceActorValue]] *[[GetBaseActorValue]] *[[ModActorValue]] *[[RestoreActorValue]] *[[SetActorValue]] *[[GetActorValueModifier]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Actor Value Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns whether a live explosive has been planted on an actor. |name = GetPlantedExplosive |returnType = bool |referenceType = [actor:ref] |example = if BuddyRef.GetPlantedExplosive }}
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Actor State Functions]] [[Category:Projectile Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = GetPlayerControlsDisabled returns true (1) if any of the specified player controls are currently disabled, and false (0) otherwise. |returnType = bool |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Movement |Type = bool |Optional = true }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Pipboy |Type = bool |Optional = true }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Fighting |Type = bool |Optional = true }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = POV |Type = bool |Optional = true }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Looking |Type = bool |Optional = true }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = RolloverText |Type = bool |Optional = true }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Sneaking |Type = bool |Optional = true }} |example = GetPlayerControlsDisabled Returns 1 if any of the player's controls are currently disabled. GetPlayerControlsDisabled 0 1 0 0 0 Returns 1 if the player's Pip-Boy is currently disabled. DisablePlayerControls 0 1 0 1 0 ; will disable pipboy and POV switch. GetPlayerControlsDisabled 0 1 0 0 0 ; returns 1 GetPlayerControlsDisabled 0 0 0 1 0 ; returns 1 GetPlayerControlsDisabled 0 1 0 1 0 ; returns 1 GetPlayerControlsDisabled 1 1 1 1 1 ; returns 1 GetPlayerControlsDisabled 0 0 0 0 0 ; returns 0 GetPlayerControlsDisabled 1 0 1 0 1 ; returns 0 }} ==Notes==
  • Default (no parameters) is equivalent to: 
    GetPlayerControlsDisabled 1 1 1 1 1 1 1  ; Return true if any controls are currently disabled.
    The function returns true if ANY of the specified controls are currently disabled (see examples).
==See Also== *[[EnablePlayerControls]] *[[DisablePlayerControls]] *[[GetRestrained]] *[[GetTFC]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Player Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns a reference to the object ref the player is currently grabbing (if any). |name = GetPlayerGrabbedRef |returnVal = grabbed |returnType = ref |example = set refVar to GetPlayerGrabbedRef }} ==See Also== *[[OnGrab]] *[[OnRelease]] *[[IsPlayerGrabbedRef]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Reference Variable Functions]] [[Category:Player Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = '''GetPlayerName''' opens the player name entry menu. |name = GetPlayerName }} ==Notes== *This open a text-entry menu for the player to input their name. It does not actually get/return what their name is set to. ==See Also== *[[ShowNameMenu]] - functionally the same. [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Player Functions]] [[Category:Name Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns 1 if calling actor is set as a player teammate. |name = GetPlayerTeammate |returnType = bool |referenceType = [actor:ref] |example = BobREF.GetPlayerTeammate }} ==See Also== *[[SetPlayerTeammate]] *[[GetPlayerTeammateCount]] *[[GetTeammates]] *[[OpenTeammateContainer]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category: Functions]] [[Category: Player Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Follower Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns the current number of actors set as player teammates. |name = GetPlayerTeammateCount |returnType = int }} ==See Also== *[[GetPlayerTeammate]] *[[SetPlayerTeammate]] *[[GetTeammates]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category: Functions]] [[Category: Player Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Follower Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns 1 if the [[quest]] has been marked complete with [[CompleteQuest]] OR has been failed, otherwise 0. |name = GetQuestCompleted |alias = GetQC |returnType = int |returnVal = IsCompleted |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = QuestID |Type = ref |Optional = true }} |example = GetQuestCompleted QuestName }}
==Notes== * This function does not require the quest to be running, and will return true even if the quest has been halted using [[StopQuest]] or similar, so long as the quest has also been marked complete using [[CompleteQuest]]. * To work around how this returns true if the quest was completed via failure, [[GetQuestFailed]] can be used to differentiate how the quest was completed.
==See Also== *[[CompleteQuest]] *[[FailQuest]] *[[GetObjectiveCompleted]] *[[GetQuestCompletedAlt]] *[[GetQuestFailed]] *[[StartQuest]] *[[StopQuest]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Quest Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |alias = GetQR |summary = Returns 1 if the quest is currently running, 0 if it is not. |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = questname |Type = EditorID }} }} ==Examples== GetQuestRunning SQ09 GetQR SQ09 ; The alias version of the function. Tests whether the quest with the EditorID of SQ09 is running.
==See Also== *[[StartQuest]] *[[StopQuest]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Quest Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns the value of a quest variable. |name = GetQuestVariable |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = QuestID |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = VarName |Type = String }} }} ==Notes== *This is ONLY a condition function. In order to get the value of a global variable in a script, there is no need for a function. Just use the syntax:
QuestID.VarName
==See Also== *[[GetGlobalValue]] *[[GetScriptVariable]] *[[GetStage]] *[[ShowQuestVars]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Quest Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns the radiation level in the specified actor's current environment. |name = GetRadiationLevel |returnVal = rads |returnType = float |example = if player.GetRadiationLevel == 100 Condition is true if the player is currently in an area with a radiation level of 100. }} ==Notes== * The radiation level is directly proportional to your rads/sec meter. So a radiation level of 3 would give you 3 rads/sec. * To get the current radiation value of an actor use [[GetAV]] '''radiationrads'''. ==See Also== *[[GetRadiationLevelAlt]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Actor State Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |CSWikiPage = GetRandomPercent |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns a random integer between 0 and 99 inclusive. |name = GetRandomPercent |returnVal = 0 to 99 |returnType = int |example = 
short rnd
set rnd to GetRandomPercent
This will set the variable "rnd" to an integer between 0 and 99, inclusive }} ==Notes== *To generate a random number between 1 and max: 
set randVal to 1 + GetRandomPercent * max / 100
To generate a random number between min and max: 
set randVal to min + GetRandomPercent * (max-min+1) / 100
==See Also== *[[GetRandomInRange]] *[[Rand]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Math Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns 1 if the actor has been set to a restrained state using [[SetRestrained]] and 0 otherwise |name = GetRestrained |returnVal = 0, 1 |returnType = int |referenceType = [Actor] |example = 
BuddyRef.GetRestrained
Returns a 1 if Buddy has been set to restrained }} ==Notes== *Restrained actors will not move from their current position, but will continue to "think" (pick packages, run detection so they could yell alarms) and go into dialogue. ==See Also== *[[SetRestrained]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:AI_Functions]] [[Category:Condition_Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = '''GetScale''' returns the raw scale of the selected reference object. Normal scale is 1.00 (100%). |name = GetScale |returnVal = scale |returnType = float |referenceType = [Object] |example = 
ShackDoor.GetScale
Will return the shack door's current scale }} ==See Also== *[[SetScale]] *[[GetBaseScale]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Object_Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Scale Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns the value of a local variable in the specified object's script. |name = GetScriptVariable |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ObjectRef |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = VarName |Type = String }} }} ==Notes== * This is ONLY a condition function. In order to get the value of a quest variable in a script, there is no need for a function. Just use the syntax: '''ObjectRef.VarName'''
==See Also== *[[GetGlobalValue]] *[[GetQuestVariable]] *[[ShowQuestVars]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |name = GetSecondsPassed |summary = Returns the number of real life seconds that have passed since this function was last called by the calling script. |returnVal = seconds |returnType = float |example = 
float timer

begin gamemode
   if timer < 5
      set timer to timer + GetSecondsPassed
   else
      ;5 seconds have passed, do something special
      set timer to 0
   endif
end
}} ==Notes== *[[SetGlobalTimeMultiplier]] '''will''' affect the return value of this function in the same way it affects the speed of the game. **Divide '''GetSecondsPassed''''s result by [[GetGlobalTimeMultiplier]] to get the time passed in real-life time. *Each script has its own independent tracking of when GetSecondsPassed was last called. *Calling this function multiple times in the same script in the same frame will return the same values for each call. *This function is unreliable in a ScriptEffect during sleep/wait/fast travel. Use [[ScriptEffectElapsedSeconds]] instead. *To know how many in-game days have passed, use GameDaysPassed instead (see [[Special Variables]]). [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Time Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |CSWikiPage = GetSelf |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns the RefID of the calling reference. [[GetSelf]] is only useful when this information is accessed externally, or when the scripted reference's RefID needs to be passed as a parameter to a function like [[PushActorAway]] Returns null if there is no valid calling reference, OR if the calling reference happens to be a non-Persistent [[Item]]. This latter property can cause issues with scripts that run on items; see [[GetSelfAlt]] for a safer alternative. |alias = this |example = ref rSelf set rSelf to GetSelf player.PushActorAway rSelf 5 }} ==Notes==
  • GetSelf shouldn't be used in object scripts on an item that is or can be in inventory.
  • While functions that take an implied reference can be cast without needing a variable or call to getself, like so: 
    CIOS someSpell
    Doing so may not be clear, and makes the code less portable if moved to a different script that does not support the implied reference. For this reason, modders should prefer the more expressive syntax: 
    ref rSelf
set rSelf to GetSelf
rSelf.CIOS someSpell
    Not only for [[CIOS]], but for all "reference methods" that require a reference, like CIOS.
  • [[GetSelf]] will return 0 if called on a reference that has been created dynamically (for example, created via [[PlaceAtMe]], or dropped from an inventory into the game world).
*Using "GetSelf" in scripts rather than "this" will result in GECKPU throwing errors. ==See Also== *[[GetSelfAlt]] - will always return the calling reference, even if it happens to be a non-persistent [[Item]]. *[[GetActionRef]] *[[GetContainer]] *[[GetIsID]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Reference Variable Functions]] [[Category:ID Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = [[GetSitting]] returns a value based on the calling actors current sitting state. |returnType = int |referenceType = [Actor] |Example = FreddyRef.GetSitting Returns FreddyRefs sitting state. }} ==Return Values== 
0	Not sitting
1	Loading sitting idle
2	Getting ready to sit
3	Is sitting
4	Getting ready to stand up
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns a value based on the actor's current sleeping state |name = GetSleeping |returnVal = 0, 1, 2, 3, 4 |returnType = int |referenceType = [Actor] |example = 
BuddyRef.GetSleeping
Will return Buddy's current sleeping state }} ==Return Values== 
0	Not sleeping 
1	Loading sleeping idle 
2	Getting ready to sleep 
3	Is sleeping 
4	Getting ready to wake up
==Notes== * This will NOT work on the player. Use [[IsPCSleeping]] instead. ==See Also== * [[IsPCSleeping]] * [[GetSitting]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Actor State Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Gets the highest completed quest stage. For example, if stages 10, 30, and 75 were completed, GetStage would return 75. Even when stage 30 is completed after stage 75, GetStage will still return 75 (i.e. the highest one). If you need to know if a certain quest stage is completed, use [[GetStageDone]] instead. |name = GetStage |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = questID |Type = form }} }} ==See Also== * [[SetStage]] * [[GetStageDone]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Quest Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns 1 if the specified stage for the specified [[Quest]] has been completed, 0 otherwise. |name = GetStageDone |returnType = bool |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = questID |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = questStage |Type = int }} }} ==See Also== * [[GetStage]] * [[SetStage]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Quest Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns the original world rotation angle of the object on the selected axis (X, Y, or Z) in float. The angle is the angle the object was placed in the editor, not it's current game state. |name = GetStartingAngle |returnType = float |referenceType = [Object] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = axis |Value = X, Y, Z }} |example = 
MyObject.GetStartingAngle z
Will return the original Z angle of ''MyObject''. }} ==See Also== *[[GetAngle]] *[[GetStartingPos]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Movement Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns the original world position of the object on the selected axis (X, Y, or Z) in float. The returned position is the position the object was placed in the editor, not it's current game state. |name = GetStartingPos |returnType = float |referenceType = [Object] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = axis |Value = x, y, z }} |example = 
ShackDoor.GetStartingPos z
This will return the shack door's original z position }} ==See Also== *[[GetPos]] *[[GetStartingAngle]] *[[MoveToEditorPosition]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Movement Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns 1 if the NPC has ever spoken to the player, 0 otherwise. |name = GetTalkedToPC |returnVal = 0, 1 |returnType = int |referenceType = [Actor] |example = 
BuddyRef.GetTalkedToPC
Will return a 1 if Buddy has ever talked to the player, and 0 otherwise. }} [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Actor State Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns the calculated threat ratio between two specified actors. |name = GetThreatRatio |returnType = float |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = actor |Type = ref }} |example = set fThreat to Settler01REF.GetThreatRatio Player Function returns the threat ratio between Settler01 and the Player. }} ==See Also== * [[Confidence]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns how long (in hours) the actor has been dead. Returns 0 if the actor is invalid, it isn't dead, or if it isn't loaded / has been [[Disable|disabled]].
If the actor gets disabled after dying, it loses its info about when it has died, meaning GetTimeDead returns 0 even if the actor is re-[[Enable|enabled]]. |name = GetTimeDead |returnType = float |referenceType = [Actor] |example = 
BuddyRef.GetTimeDead
Returns how many hours Buddy has been dead, in float form. }} ==See Also== *[[GetDead]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Condition_Functions]] [[Category:Time Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns the number of warnings an actor on a [[Guard Package]] (or responding to trespassing or responding to an aggro radius violation) has given the specified reference. |name = GetTrespassWarningLevel |returnType = int |referenceType = [actor:ref] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = actor |Type = ref }} |example = FlashRef.GetTrespassWarningLevel Player Returns the number of times Flash has issued a warning to the player. }} ==See Also== *[[iGuardWarnings]], for the maximum number of warnings a guard will issue.
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns 1 if the actor has been set to an unconscious state using the [[SetUnconscious]] function. |name = GetUnconscious |returnVal = 0, 1 |returnType = int |referenceType = [Actor] |example = 
BuddyRef.GetUnconscious
Returns 1 if Buddy has been set unconscious }} ==Notes== *This function does not detect actors who have been rendered unconscious from fatigue damage or from being essential and losing all their health. To detect these situations, use [[GetKnockedState]] intead. ==See Also== *[[SetUnconscious]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Condition_Functions]] [[Category:Actor_State_Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Incomplete}}{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns true if the actor is using/activating an item(?) |name = GetUsedItemActivate |returnVal = 0, 1 |returnType = int |referenceType = [Actor] |example = 
BuddyRef.GetUsedItemActivate
Returns 1 if Buddy is activating something, and 0 otherwise(?) }} ==Notes== *This condition function is only used in the default game as a conditional for Idle Animations, to check whether an actor is activating something ==See Also== *[[GetIsUsedItem]] *[[GetIsUsedItemType]] *[[GetIsUsedItemEquipType]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Condition_Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Incomplete}}
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:VATS Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Incomplete}}
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:VATS Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Incomplete}}
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:VATS Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Incomplete}}
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:VATS Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Incomplete}}
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:VATS Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Incomplete}}
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:VATS Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns the player's current VATS view. |name = GetVATSMode |returnType = int }} ==Return Types== 
0	VATS_MODE_NONE
1	VATS_TARGET_SELECT
2	VATS_MENU_MODE
3	VATS_SCAN_MODE
4	VATS_PLAYBACK
==See Also== *[[IsInKillCam]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:VATS Functions]] [[Category:Player Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Incomplete}}
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:VATS Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Incomplete}}
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:VATS Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = The [[GetVATSValue]] condition function is available for use either when selecting a camera path, or within the Calculate To-Hit Chance entry point. Outside of VATS playback mode or Calculate To-Hit Chance, or if used in the console, the function will not return a meaningful value. When you select GetVATSValue, you have one or two additional parameters to select, the Query and an optional parameter to the Query. When used within the Calculate To-Hit Chance entry point, remember that the command has not actually taken place yet, so things like Success/Failure, critical strike info, dismembered/exploded limb or total damage are not calculated yet. However, the weapon, target, and targeted limb are available. |name = GetVATSValue |returnType = [varies] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = query |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = parameter |Type = varies |Optional = y }} }} =='''GetVATSValue Queries'''== ''Name:'' '''WeaponIs'''
''Parameter:'' Choose a specific weapon from the drop-down list
''Returns:'' 1 if the player used the specified weapon to attack, 0 if not.
''Name:'' '''Weapon In List'''
''Parameter:'' Choose a FormList that consists ONLY of Weapons
''Returns:'' 1 if the weapon the player used is in the specified list, 0 if not.
''Name:'' '''Weapon Type Is'''
''Parameter:'' Choose a Weapon Anim Type from the drop-down list
''Returns:'' 1 if the weapon is set to the specified type, 0 if not.
''Name:'' '''Target Is'''
''Parameter:'' Choose a specific NPC or Creature from the drop-down list
''Returns:'' 1 if the target is the chosen NPC or Creature, 0 if not
''Name:'' '''Target In List'''
''Parameter:'' Choose a FormList that consists ONLY of NPCs and/or Creatures
''Returns:'' 1 if the target is in the specified list, 0 if not.
''Name:'' '''Target Distance'''
''Parameter:'' N/A
''Returns:'' The distance to the target in units.
''Name:'' '''Targeted Part'''
''Parameter:'' A Condition actor value corresponding to the desired body part
''Returns:'' 1 if the player is targeting the specified part, 0 if not.
''Name:'' '''VATS Action'''
''Parameter:'' A VATS action, such as Attack, Reload, Throw Grenade, etc.
''Returns:'' 1 if the current VATS action is the one specified, 0 if not.
''Name:'' '''Is Success'''
''Parameter:'' N/A
''Returns:'' 1 if the VATS action is successful (made the die roll), 0 if not.
''Name:'' '''Is Critical'''
''Parameter:'' N/A
''Returns:'' 1 if the VATS action results in a critical strike, 0 if not
''Name:'' '''Crit Effect Is'''
''Parameter:'' Choose from a list of self-targeted spells
''Returns:'' 1 if the VATS action results in a critical strike and the critical effect matches the one selected, 0 if not.
''Name:'' '''Crit Effect In List'''
''Parameter:'' Choose a Form List containing ONLY self-targeted spells.
''Returns:'' 1 if the VATS action results in a critical strike and the critical effect is in the specified list, 0 if not.
''Name:'' '''Is Fatal'''
''Parameter:'' N/A
''Returns:'' 1 if the VATS action results in the target's death, 0 if not.
''Name:'' '''Explode Part'''
''Parameter:'' N/A
''Returns:'' 1 if the VATS action results in a body part exploding, 0 if not.
''Name:'' '''Dismember Part'''
''Parameter:'' N/A
''Returns:'' 1 if the VATS action results in a body part being dismembered, 0 if not.
''Name:'' '''Cripple Part'''
''Parameter:'' N/A
''Returns:'' 1 if the VATS action results in a body part being crippled (condition changes from > 0 to <= 0), 0 if not.
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:VATS Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns the [[Actor]]'s walk speed |name = GetWalkSpeed |returnType = float |referenceType = [Actor] |example = 
Player.GetWalkSpeed
Will return the player's walk speed }} ==See Also== *[[GetRunSpeed]] *[[SetSpeedMult]] *[[Movement Settings]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Condition_Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns a number corresponding to the actor's current weapon animation type. See notes for return values. |name = GetWeaponAnimType |returnType = int |referenceType = [actor:ref] |example = if BuddyRef.GetWeaponAnimType == 4 }} =='''Return Values and Animation Types'''==
Internally, weapons in ''Fallout 3'' and ''Fallout: New Vegas'' have a total of 13 or 14 different possible animation types, respectively. However, GetWeaponAnimType can only discern 11 different animation types, with one catch-all for unrecognized values. Certain animation types are combined into one return value, as described below: {{Weapon_Animation_Types}}
==Notes== * Function was created for the VATS camera conditions
==See Also== * [[GetWeaponType]]
<!-- [[GetWeaponAnimType_Reference|List of functions that use GetWeaponAnimType]] (wiki) -->
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Animation Functions]] [[Category:Engine Differences (Fallout)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns the condition level (health) of an actor's currently equipped weapon. |name = GetWeaponHealthPerc |returnType = float |referenceType = [actor:ref] |example = If BruceRef.GetWeaponHealthPerc < 50 Checks to see if Bruce's equipped weapon is below 50% of its base condition. }} ==See Also== *[[SetWeaponHealthPerc]] *[[ModWeaponHealthPerc]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns wind speed of current weather (ranges from 0.0 to 1.0, as set on the Weather type in the editor). |name = GetWindSpeed |returnVal = 0.0 to 1.0 |returnType = float |example = 
GetWindSpeed
Will return the current wind speed }} [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Condition_Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns 1 if the calling actor has been eaten by the player, which can only happen if the player is a cannibal. Used as a condition function in the Cannibal [[:Category:Perk|Perk]].
This function is viable for use in scripts, but further testing is needed to determine whether it can be used well after the actor has been consumed. |name = HasBeenEaten |returnType = bool |referenceType = [actor:ref] }} ==See Also== *[[DonnerReedKuruParty]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns 1 if the actor who it is called on is equal or greater to the friend disposition game setting. It is called on a Reference and takes no parameters. It is also a Condition Function. |name = HasFriendDisposition |returnType = int |referenceType = [actor:ref] |example = FreddyRef.HasFriendDispostion Will return 1 if Freddy has the friend disposition or 0 if not. }} ==See Also== * [[CheckSpeechDispositionBoost]] * [[FriendDispositionBoost]] * [[SetDisposition]]
<!-- [[HasFriendDisposition_Reference|List of functions that use HasFriendDisposition]] (wiki) -->
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns 1 if the calling reference currently has 3D loaded. |name = HasLoaded3D |returnVal = 0, 1 |returnType = bool |example = Ref.HasLoaded3D HasLoaded3D }}
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]] [[Category:Condition Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns whether or not the calling [[Actor]] has the specified [[Perk]]. *When called on the [[Player]] character, this function will return whether the player has the perk, unless the optional argument '''forTeammates''' is non-zero, in which case it will return whether the player's teammates have the perk. *When called on any actor other than the player, this function will work correctly only if the '''uNPCPerks''' optional [https://www.nexusmods.com/newvegas/mods/58277 JIP LN] feature is toggled in '''Data\NVSE\plugins\jip_nvse.ini'''. *When called via the console, this function will also print the current perk rank. |name = HasPerk |returnType = hasPerk:0/1 |referenceType = ActorRef |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = perk |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = forTeammates |Type = 1/0 |Optional = y }} |example = 
set bHasGunNut to player.HasPerk GunNut
Returns whether or not the '''Player''' has the "Gun Nut" perk. 
set bHasGunNut to player.HasPerk GunNut 1
Returns whether or not the '''Player's teammates''' have the "Gun Nut" perk. 
set bHasGunNut to ActorRef.HasPerk GunNut
Returns whether or not the '''ActorRef''' has the "Gun Nut" perk. }} ==See Also== *[[AddPerk]] *[[RemovePerk]] *[[GetPerkRank]] *[[GetAltPerkRank]] *[[HasPerkRank]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Player Functions]] [[Category:Perk Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = '''IgnoreCrime''' Prevents an NPC from attacking or reporting a player for crime. |name = IgnoreCrime |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ignoreCrimeFlag |Type = bool }} |example = RobertRef.IgnoreCrime 1 This makes it so that Robert won't attack the player or report crimes the player commits. }} ==Notes== *If the actor is not a friend or ally of the player, he will still react to being directly attacked (but will not report it as a crime). *The values for the flag mean: 
0	Report crimes the player commits (default).
1	Do not report crimes the player commits.
==See Also== *[[GetIgnoreCrime]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Crime Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]
