==See Also== *[[SayTo]] *[[SayToDone]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Dialogue Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |name = SayTo |summary = '''SayTo''' makes an actor say a single line of dialogue to another actor. The function returns 0, so setting a variable equal to the return value will not do anything. To set up an action for after the line is spoken, see [[SayToDone]], which is a block that is triggered when the SAYTO is complete. |referenceType = Actor |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = targetActor |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Topic |Type = EditorID }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = forceSubtitleFlag |Type = int |Optional = Optional }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = noTargetLookFlag |Type = int |Optional = Optional }} |example = 
SayTo JoeRef SecretTopic
SayTo player SecretTopic 1
force subtitles 
SayTo JoeRef SecretTopic 0 1
Joe is not forced to look at the speaker during the SayTo }} ==Bugs== * Using SayTo in a package result script will cause the package to hang if the player is not in the cell ==See Also== *[[Say]] *[[SayToDone]] *[[SayTo_GetTopic]] / [[SayTo_GetTopicInfo]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Dialogue Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Available only in Effect Scripts. Returns seconds elapsed since the last magic effect update. This will only return a value greater than 0 inside a [[ScriptEffectUpdate]] block ('''but it will compile without error in other block types'''). |name = ScriptEffectElapsedSeconds |returnVal = elapsedSeconds |returnType = float |example = 
float fTimer
 set fTimer to fTimer + ScriptEffectElapsedSeconds
}} [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category: Functions]] [[Category:Game Effect Functions]] [[Category:Time Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Manually creates an assault crime. There are two optional parameters: an actor reference (the specific victim, if applicable - if not applicable, use player) and a faction reference. |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = OffendedActor |Type = ref |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = OffendedFaction |Type = ref |Optional = y }} |referenceType = ref |example = 
Player.SendAssaultAlarm FlashRef
Flash will react as if he is a victim of an assault crime. 
SendAssaultAlarm Player BigTown
Because the player is not a valid target for an assault alarm, it will pick an NPC in the BigTown faction to use instead. }} ==Notes== *This command CANNOT be used to have ai actors attack other ai, The [[player]] is always considered the one committing the crime. *Causes actors to yell out the "Assault" [[dialogue]] topic even if they are already in combat with the target. ==Bugs== *Using this command without a calling REF can sometimes lead to a CTD. ==See Also== *[[ClearActorsFactionsPlayerEnemyFlag]] *[[ClearFactionPlayerEnemyFlag]] *[[GetActorFactionPlayerEnemy]] *[[GetActorCrimePlayerEnemy]] *[[SendTrespassAlarm]] *[[SendStealingAlarm]] *[[StartCombat]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Crime Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Sends out a trespass alarm as if someone had reported the player trespassing. |name = SendTrespassAlarm |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = reportedTo |Type = ref |Optional = y }} |referenceType = [reportedTo:Ref] |example = 
SendTrespassAlarm SunnyRef
Alerts Sunny that the player trespassed. 
SunnyRef.SendTrespassAlarm player
Alerts Sunny that the player trespassed. }} ==Notes== * Seems to only take the player as the criminal parameter. * Goes all the way to fully trespassed on one call, regardless of [[IGuardWarnings|iGuardWarnings]] GMST. ==See Also== *[[SendAssaultAlarm]] *[[SendStealingAlarm]] *[[SetCellPublicFlag]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Crime Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Sets the actor's alpha value (how transparent they are).
NOTE: Does not affect the weapon they carry. |name = SetActorAlpha |alias = SAA |referenceType = [actor:ref] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = alpha |Type = float }} |example = SetActorAlpha 0.5 }} ==See Also== *[[GetActorAlpha]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category: Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Changes the actor's name (display name) to the title (not the text) of the specified [[Message]]. Note that while the function is called on a reference, it actually changes the name of the base object, so all instances of that NPC/creature will have a new name.
|name = SetActorFullName |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = message |Type = baseform }} |example = ref.SetActorFullName NamedActorHarold }} ==Notes== *SetActorFullName can also be used on a [[TalkingActivator|talking activator]].
==See Also== *[[SetFullNameAlt]] *[[SetName]] *[[LNSetName]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Name Functions]]

{{Function |CSWikiPage = SetActorValue |origin = GECK1 |summary = SetActorValue sets the stat's base value to the new value.
See [[Stats List]] for a list of stats that can be accessed using SetActorValue.
See [[ActorValue]] to understand how Actor Values are calculated. |name = SetActorValue |alias = SetAV |referenceType = Actor |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = StatName |Type = actorValue }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Value |Type = int }} |example = Player.SetActorValue Health 3 SetActorValue Strength 6 }}
== See Also == *[[UnsetAV]] to undo this function's effects. *[[ForceActorValue]] *[[GetActorValue]] *[[GetActorValueInfo]] *[[GetBaseActorValue]] *[[GetPermanentActorValue]] *[[ModActorValue]] *[[RestoreActorValue]] *[[Stats List]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Actor Value Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = '''SetActorsAI ''' turns on and off the processing of an actor in the world. It is called on a reference and takes one parameter an integer. If the integer is 0, it turns off the processing and any other integer will turn it on. Scripts will still run on them but they will not process packages, detection, Havok or animation. |name = SetActorsAI |referenceType = [actor:ref] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = processAI |Type = bool }} |example = BuddyRef.SetActorsAI 0 Buddy will no longer process schedules, detection, Havok or animation. }} ==See Also== *[[ToggleActorsAI]] *[[SetRestrained]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:AI Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Sets whether the actor is in an "alert" state (weapon drawn). This is purely an animation state, and does not affect their actual combat status. |name = SetAlert |returnType = void |referenceType = actor |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = value |Type = bool}} }} ==Notes== *This function is savebaked and in addition is not cleared after any length of time or when a [[cell]] resets. *using [[UnEquipItem]] and it's variants at the same time you are setting the alert state to 1 OR 0 can cause the actor be stuck holding an invisible weapon.
==See Also== *[[GetIsAlerted]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:AI Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Savebaked Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = This is one of 4 functions which can be used to increase the game's performance in situations where many actors are in combat or otherwise doing complex pathing. Note that the flag is cleared every frame, so this function needs to be called in a script running on a reference in the loaded area.
When the SetAllVisible flag is set, actors do not perform LineOfSight checks. When actors check to see if they can see something, they assume they can see its head. |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = enable |Type = int }} |example = SetAllVisible 1 }}
==See Also== *[[SetSceneIsComplex]] *[[SetAllReachable]] *[[SetNoAvoidance]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category: Functions]] [[Category: AI Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Sets a friend/ally relationship between two factions. It is not called on a reference. Will add the second argument faction to the first argument faction's reaction list if it is absent from the list. |name = SetAlly |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = faction1 |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = faction2 |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = F1toF2Flag |Type = int |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = F2toF1Flag |Type = int |Optional = y }} |example = 
SetAlly BrotherhoodSteelFaction RivetCityFaction
This makes the BrotherhoodSteelFaction and the RivetCityFaction allies with each other 
SetAlly BrotherhoodSteelFaction RivetCityFaction 0 1
This makes the BrotherhoodSteelFaction an Ally of RivetCityFaction RivetCityFaction a Friend to BrotherhoodSteelFaction }} ==Notes== *The values for the flag mean: **0 = Ally (default) **1 = Friend ==See Also== * [[SetEnemy]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Faction Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Sets the angle ('''in degrees''') of the calling object relative to the world on the specified axis (X, Y, or Z). |name = SetAngle |returnType = void |referenceType = [Object] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Axis |Type = char |Value = X, Y, Z }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Angle |Type = float }} |example = ActorRef.SetAngle Z 270 Player.SetAngle X 28.8 }} ==Notes== When called on the player and actors: *SetAngle Z rotates the player/actor as it rotates any object in game. It can be used to force the player or actor face a certain direction. E.g. In an exterior cells, "Player.SetAngle Z 45" forces the player face NE.
*Player and actors cannot be tilted forward, backward or sideways like most other objects, therefore changing their X and Y angles has no effect, with the following exception.
*SetAngle X on the player tilts the camera view up/down. It is possible to set the player's X angle beyond the limits of the mouse movement (-89 to 89). Note that the values are counterintuitive: negative angles force the player look up, positive angles, down.
*Some objects will 'forget' the X angle, setting it back to zero every few frames.
*Statics may not respond to SetAngle unless spawned dynamically via [[PlaceAtMe]].
==See Also== *[[SetAngleEx]] *[[GetAngle]] *[[GetHeadingAngle]] *[[GetPos]] / [[SetPos]] *[[MoveTo]] *[[Rotate]] *[[Turn]] *[[FaceObject]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Movement Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |name = SetBroadcastState |summary = Sets the broadcast state of a radio station. |referenceType = [radio:ref] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = broadcastState |Type = int }} |example = NPCRadio.SetBroadcastState 1 NPCRadio will now broadcast whatever audio is assigned to that station. }} ==Broadcast State== 
0	turns off continuous broadcast -- the station will play static except when playing scripted conversations using StartRadioConversation.
1	turns on continuous broadcast -- the station will generate and play radio conversations one after another.
==See Also== *[[GetBroadcastState]] *[[StartRadioConversation]] *[[:Category:Radio Stations|Radio Stations]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Radio Stations]] [[Category:Radio Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Changes the cell's display name to the '''title''' text of the specified [[message]]. This is what will appear on load doors leading into the cell, as well as on the local map. |name = SetCellFullName |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = cellID |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = messageID |Type = baseform }} |example = SetCellFullName HouseForSale HouseForSaleMsg }} ==Notes== * Only works with interior cells.
==See Also== *[[SetName]] *[[SetNameEx]] *[[LNSetName]] *[[SetFullNameAlt]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category: Functions]] [[Category: Miscellaneous Functions]] [[Category:Name Functions]] [[Category:Cell Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Changes the ImageSpace of a cell. |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Cell |Type = EditorID }}{{FunctionArgument |ImageSpace |Type = EditorID }} |example = SetCellImageSpace Vault101a VaultImageSpace01 Sets the ImageSpace of the cell ''Vault101a'' to ''VaultImageSpace01'' }} ==Notes== *After calling [[SetCellImageSpace]] on a cell that is currently loaded, the ImageSpace will not be refreshed until the cell is reloaded. This will happen if the player moves away from the cell then back to the cell, or if a save made in the cell (with the new ImageSpace) is loaded from the menu. This is fixed in [[ButcherPete FOSE]] for Fallout 3 and JohnnyGuitar NVSE version 5.18 for New Vegas. ==See Also== *[[GetCellImageSpace]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Cell Functions]] [[Category:Imagespace Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Sets the ownership of the specified [[Cell]] to the specified [[NPC]] or [[Faction]] BaseForm. If the second parameter is left blank, the cell will be set as owned by the [[Player]]. |name = SetCellOwnership |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = cell |Type = BaseForm }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = newOwner |Type = BaseForm |Optional = y }} |example = 
SetCellOwnership WeynonPrioryHouse BladesFaction
WeynonPrioryHouse set to BladesFaction-owned. 
SetCellOwnership MyImperialCityHouse
MyImperialCityHouse set to player-owned. }} ==See Also== *[[GetOwnerOfCell]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Crime Functions]] [[Category:Cell Functions]] [[Category:Ownership Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Sets the cell's public flag (1 or 0). Cells which are public cannot have trespassing, so this is useful for changing the [[:Category:Crime|Crime]] behavior in cells. |name = SetCellPublicFlag |alias = SetPublic |returnType = traitValue:float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = cellID |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = publicFlag |Type = int }} |example = SetCellPublicFlag MyCell 1 }} ==See Also== *[[IsTrespassing]] *[[SendTrespassAlarm]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Crime Functions]] [[Category:Cell Functions]] [[Category:Flag Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Changes the [[Class|class]] of the actor to the parameter. An autocalc is performed, if and only if the checkbox is marked for the actor. It is called on a reference. |name = SetClass |referenceType = [actor:ref] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = class |Type = baseform }} |example = BuddyRef.SetClass VaultDweller }} ==See Also== *[[GetIsClass]] *[[GetClass]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Sets the calling actor reference's [[Combat_Style|combat style]] to the new style specified. |name = SetCombatStyle |referenceType = [actor:ref] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = CombatStyleID |Type = baseform }} }} ==Notes== *Does not change the combat style on the base form of the actor but instead only changes the combat style of the calling reference.
==See Also== *[[GetCombatStyle]] *[[SetCombatStyleFlag]] *[[SetCombatStyleTraitNumeric]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category: Functions]] [[Category: Combat Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Sets the reference to be in the given critical stage and applies its effects. |name = SetCriticalStage |referenceType = ref |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = eCritStage |Type = string }} |example = SetCriticalStage GooStart }} ==Notes== eCritStage values are: * None * GooStart -- change constraints in the ragdoll * GooEnd -- take ref out of the water system, cull its 3D, and remove its Havok data from the world * DisintegrateStart -- change constraints in the ragdoll * DisintegrateEnd -- take ref out of the water system, cull its 3D, and remove its Havok data from the world
==See Also== *[[IsInCriticalStage]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Sets the default open state for an object. (The equivalent of using the "Open By Default" checkbox on a reference.) |name = SetDefaultOpen |referenceType = ref |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = defaultOpen |Type = int }} |example = SetDefaultOpen 1 object's default state is now "open" }} ==See Also== * [[GetDefaultOpen]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = SetDestroyed takes an int parameter. A 1 parameter marks the ref as destroyed, a 0 parameter clears the destroyed flag on the ref. |name = SetDestroyed |referenceType = ref |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = destroyedFlag |Type = int }} |example = SetDestroyed 1 }} ==Notes== *This can be used on any door or activator to indicate that it is non-functional. Destroyed doors/activators will no longer give rollover text and cannot be activated. *This should not be used on temporary references as the flag appears to not be cleared if the formid is reused. *Using this function on actors is not recommended, use a script instead to make activating them do nothing. *Do not use this function on spawn markers, it will create various bugs including immunity to explosives.
==See Also== *[[GetDestroyed]] *[[SetInteractionDisabled]] *[[Destructible_Object_Data]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = [[SetEnemy]] sets an enemy/neutral relationship between two factions. It is not called on a reference. Will add the second argument faction to the first argument faction's reaction list if it is absent from the list. |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Faction1 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Faction2 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = F1toF2Flag |Value = 0,1 |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = F2toF1Flag |Value = 0,1 |Optional = y }} }} ==Examples==
SetEnemy BrotherhoodSteelFaction EnclaveFaction This makes the BrotherhoodSteelFaction and the EnclaveFaction Enemies of each other SetEnemy BrotherhoodSteelFaction EnclaveFaction 1 0 This makes the BrotherhoodSteelFaction Neutral to EnclaveFaction but EnclaveFaction will treat BrotherhoodSteelFaction as an Enemy
==Notes== *The values for the flag mean: **0 = Enemy (default) **1 = Neutral
==See Also== * [[SetAlly]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Faction Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |CSWikiPage = SetEssential |summary = Sets the essential state of the actor. |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = BaseID |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Flag |Type = boolean }} |example = SetEssential MyCompanionID 1
Sets the object, referred to by MyCompanionID, to essential. }} ==See Also== *[[IsEssential]] *[[SetActorRefEssential]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Savebaked Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Sets the actor's rank in the specified [[:Category:Factions|faction]]. If the actor is not a member of the faction, adds them to the faction at the new rank. |name = SetFactionRank |referenceType = Actor |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = FactionID |Type = faction }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = newRank |Type = int |Optional = y }} |example = KarenSchenzyREF.SetFactionRank ArefuFaction 1 Sets Karen to rank one in the ArefuFaction faction. Player.SetFactionRank ArefuFaction -1 This will remove the player from the ArefuFaction faction. }} ==Notes== The faction rank is set on the actor's [[Glossary#B|base object]] as well as on the actor ([[reference]]) itself. Any actor spawned from the same base object (by a [[Leveled Creature|leveled list]], by [[PlaceAtMe]] and so on) will have this faction, at the same rank, already set. This is not a problem for named characters (of which there is usually just one in the world), but ''can'' be a problem for random, unnamed characters or creatures.
==See also== * [[GetFactionRank]] * [[GetFactionRankDifference]] * [[ModFactionRank]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Faction Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Set the specified faction's reaction towards the TargetFactionID to the specified amount. See [[:Category:Factions|Factions]] for more information on faction reaction. |name = SetFactionReaction |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = faction |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = targetFaction |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = modValue |Type = int }} |example = SetFactionReaction MegatonFaction playerFaction -20 }} ==See Also== *[[GetFactionReaction]] *[[ModFactionReaction]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category: Functions]] [[Category: Faction Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Turns force run on or off. |name = SetForceRun |referenceType = actor |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = forceRunFlag |Type = int }} }} ==See Also== *[[GetForceRun]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Actor State Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Turns force sneak on or off. |name = SetForceSneak |referenceType = actor |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = forceRunFlag |Type = int }} }} ==Notes== *This bug only seems to occur if the script is not attached to the actor that it is affecting:
If SetForceSneak 1 is called too soon after SetForceSneak 0, the calling ref will appear to not be sneaking, although [[IsSneaking]] and [[GetForceSneak]] will still return the correct values. The opposite occurs if SetForceSneak 0 is called too soon after SetForceSneak 1. *If SetForceSneak is set to 1 and used in conjunction with the [[FireWeapon]] function, then the character will stand when firing. This appears to be an apparent bug with animation groups not updating when using this function. If you wish to make a character shoot while sneaking, then it would be best to use a "Use Weapon" package. ==See Also== *[[GetForceSneak]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Actor State Functions]]

{{Function |CSWikiPage = SetGhost |origin = GECK1 |summary = Sets the [[actor]] [[reference]] to/from the "ghost" state. The changes are save-persistent. |name = SetGhost |returnType = void |referenceType = [Actor] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ghostState |Type = 0/1 }} |example = BuddyREF.SetGhost 1 BuddyREF is now in "ghost" state. BuddyREF.SetGhost 0 BuddyREF is no longer in "ghost" state (default state). }} ==Notes== *Cannot be harmed by a weapon or spell/magic effect in any way. [[Projectile]]s pass through them, *The stated chance to hit them in [[VATS]] is unchanged, despite them being impossible to hit (bug), *''Can'' inflict damage. So using ''player.SetGhost 1'' will not prevent the player from killing their enemies, *Is still able to talk and can be activated to talk, *Still collides normally with other actors and world objects, *Cannot be pickpocketed, despite the prompt still showing up. Instead, regular activation (dialog) occurs. *Though NPCs and creatures can not be damaged in this state, the player ''can''. To prevent the player from taking damage, use ToggleImmortalMode. *Enemies of this calling ref will not go into combat or detect an actor with this flag.
==See Also== *[[GetIsGhost]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Actor State Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |alias = sgtm |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = multiplier |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = keepSoundUnchanged |Type = bool |Value = 0 |Optional = y }} |summary = [[SetGlobalTimeMultiplier]] Sets the time multiplier. 1.0 is standard time, 0.5 is slow mo, and 0.25 is even slower.
If ''keepSoundUnchanged'' is set to 1 (true), then sounds will not be sped up/slowed down when the time mult is changed.
|example = sgtm .5 Now the world is in slow motion.
SetGlobalTimeMultiplier 2 1 Now the world is sped up, but sounds are not affected. }}
==Notes== *Don't pass a value of 0 to this function or you will break the game. {{Template:Time Mult Changes}} ==See Also== *[[GetGlobalTimeMultiplier]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Time Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Set to 1 so actor will ignore friendly hits. Set to 0 to clear the condition. |name = SetIgnoreFriendlyHits |referenceType = [actor:ref] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ignoreFriendlyHits |Type = int }} |example = BobREF.SetIgnoreFriendlyHits 1 }} ==See Also== *[[GetIgnoreFriendlyHits]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category: Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:AI Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Sets the player into a "character generation" state. While in this state, the player will not level up (although XP will accumulate as usual). When the player leaves this state, the appropriate number of levels will be immediately awarded. |name = SetInCharGen |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = iFlag |Type = bool |Value = 0/1 }} |example = SetInCharGen 1 }} ==See Also== * [[GetInCharGen]] * [[IsInCharGen]]
[[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Player Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Sets the item's base value to the specified amount. Note that while it is called on a reference, it actually changes the value of the reference's base object (and so will change the value of every reference of that base object). |name = SetItemValue |referenceType = [item:ref] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = value |Type = int }} }} == Notes == *The changes made by this function will be saved to save games.
==See Also== *[[SetBaseItemValue]] *[[GetItemValue]] *[[GetValueAlt]] *[[SetValueAlt]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category: Functions]] [[Category: Object Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Sets the last exterior door activated. |name = SetLastExtDoorActivated |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Door |Type = Reference }} |example = SetLastExtDoorActivated MyExteriorDoorREF Sets the last activated exterior door to MyExteriorDoorREF }} ==Notes== This function sets the last exterior door activated which has the effect of changing which worldspace map is currently shown in the Pip-Boy.
This function does NOT update the parent worldspace of the currently loaded cell. ==See Also== *[[GetPCLastExteriorDoor]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Door Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = In theory, if the LevelToPCFlag is set to 1 then the level provided is an offset to the player's level, which will not take their level above or below the provided maximum and minimum levels. In practice, the function does not work correctly when called on NPCs- SetLevel will often (if not always) set their level to the provided MinLevel, disregarding any level offset, maximum or anything else.
This function can be called on the player and will change the player's level, but it will not change not any of his stats (and the world will not "level up" with the player), so this is not recommended. Use [[RewardXP]] instead. |name = SetLevel |referenceType = [actor:ref] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = newLevel |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = levelToPCFlag |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = minLevel |Type = int |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = maxLevel |Type = int |Optional = y }} }} ==Notes== Calling SetLevel on an actor other than the player will cause that actor's inventory to be regenerated, re-evaluating any leveled lists that may make up the actor inventory. You can witness this in action by finding a random raider and calling setlevel on them via the console. They will usually end up wearing a different piece of armor, because their inventory was repopulated. This repopulation effect takes effect even if the SetLevel command did not change the actor's level.
==See Also== *[[GetLevel]] *[[RewardXP]] *[[RewardXPExact]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category: Functions]] [[Category: Actor State Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = This puts the game into a state where only load screens specifically associated with the current cell will be displayed. |name = SetLocationSpecificLoadScreensOnly |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = flag |Type = int |Value = 0/1 }} }} ==See Also== *[[GetLocationSpecificLoadScreensOnly]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Loading Screen Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Assigns a merchant container to an actor. |name = SetMerchantContainer |referenceType = [actor:ref] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = container |Type = ref }} }} ==Notes== * This function will fail if the merchant container is a non-persistent reference.
==See Also== *[[RemoveMerchantContainer]] *[[GetMerchantContainer]] *[[Adding items to vendors]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = This sets the "minimal use" flag on a door. |name = SetMinimalUse |referenceType = [door:ref] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = minimalUseFlag |Type = int |Value = 0/1 }} }}
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = SetNPCRadio designates an NPC as a [[Radio_Stations|radio]]. |name = SetNPCRadio |returnType = void |referenceType = [actor:reference] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = radioStation |Type = ref }} |example = BobRef.SetNPCRadio 1 GalaxyNewsRadioRef Bob is now a radio that is playing Galaxy News Radio. }} ==Notes== *Definitions for the 0/1 parameter: **0 = Radio is off - turns off the radio regardless of the called reference **1 = Radio is on *If the NPC is listed as the speaker of a line of dialog, he will speak it. If he is not the speaker, it plays with a filter effect to indicate it's not him. *You cannot change an NPCs currently playing station with this function without first using it to turn their radio off, for example by calling: 
 BobRef.SetNPCRadio 0 GalaxyNewsRadioRef 
 BobRef.SetNPCRadio 1 EnclaveRadioRef
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Radio Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Overrides the default NoActivation sound with a declared sound. |name = SetNoActivationSound |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Sound |Type = editorID }} |example = SetNoActivationSound QSTBabyCry ; A baby cry will now place the default NoActivation sound. }} ==Notes== *[[ClearNoActivationSound]] can be used to return the sound to the default sound.
[[Category: Functions]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category: Player Functions]] [[Category:Sound Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = This is one of 4 functions which can be used to increase the game's performance in situations where many actors are in combat or otherwise doing complex pathing. Note that the flag is cleared every frame, so this function needs to be called in a script running on a reference in the loaded area.
When the SetNoAvoidance flag is set, actors do not perform avoid box movement. Actors do not open doors or angle away from each other. |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = enable |Type = int }} |example = SetNoAvoidance 1 }} ==See Also== *[[SetSceneIsComplex]] *[[SetAllReachable]] *[[SetAllVisible]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category: Functions]] [[Category: AI Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Marks an objective completed or not. |name = SetObjectiveCompleted |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Quest |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = objectiveIndex |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = completedFlag |Type = int |Value = 0/1 }} |example = SetObjectiveCompleted MyQuest 10 1 Marks objective 10 in MyQuest as completed. }} ==Notes== * If the quest itself is not complete, AND the objective is currently being displayed, the player will receive an "Objective Completed" message. * If the quest itself is already complete, no message will be displayed (although the objective will still be marked as completed). * Completed objectives (which are also set to being displayed) show up as "greyed out" in the interface.
==See Also== *[[CompleteAllObjectives]] *[[CompleteQuest]] *[[GetObjectiveCompleted]] *[[SetObjectiveDisplayed]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Quest Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Turns a quest objective on or off. |name = SetObjectiveDisplayed |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Quest |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = objectiveIndex |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = displayedFlag |Type = int |Value = 0/1 }} |example = SetObjectiveDisplayed MyQuest 10 1 Turns on objective 10. }} ==Notes== * If this is the first objective of the quest to be displayed, the player will receive a "Quest Added" message on screen. * If the quest is not currently running, SetObjectiveDisplayed ... 1 will automatically start it. * Always remember that SetObjectiveDisplayed only accepts indices that are defined in the quest's "Quest Objectives" tab. When you try to display a quest objective that isn't defined, nothing will happen.
==See Also== *[[GetObjectiveDisplayed]] *[[SetObjectiveCompleted]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Quest Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Sets the open state of a [[Door]] to open or closed. |name = SetOpenState |returnType = void |referenceType = [Door] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = State |Type = boolean |Optional = n }} |example = SomeDoorRef.SetOpenState 1 ; Open SomeOtherDoorRef.SetOpenState 0 ; Closed }} ==Notes== * These states do NOT correspond to those of [[GetOpenState]]. * This function will work on Activators if they have Open and Close animations.
==See Also== *[[GetOpenState]] *[[SetDefaultOpen]] *'''ref.[[PlayAnimSequence]] "Open"/"Close"''' - this works on containers
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category: Miscellaneous Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Sets the ownership of the [[calling reference]] to the specified [[NPC]] or [[Faction]] BaseForm. If the parameter is omitted, the reference will be set to be owned by the [[Player]]'s BaseForm (refID 7). |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Owner |Type = BaseForm |Optional = Yes }} |example = RewardGunRef.SetOwnership
Sets the ownership of RewardGunRef to the player (so that they can take it without stealing). }} ==See Also== *[[ClearOwnership]] *[[GetOwner]] *[[GetOwnerOfCell]] / [[SetCellOwnership]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Object Functions]] [[Category:Ownership Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Sets the flag which allows the player to use Power Armor. |name = SetPCCanUsePowerArmor |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = canUse |Type = int }} }} ==Notes== * The perk '''PowerArmorTraining''' is just a dummy for display on the pipboy. Internally, the perk is actually setting '''SetPCCanUsePowerArmor 1''' through stage 80 of the '''Generic''' quest.
==See Also== *[[GetPCCanUsePowerArmor]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Player Functions]] [[Category:Armor Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Sets the enemy flag on a faction that tracks when the player commits a major crime against a crime-tracking faction. See [[:Category:Crime#Faction_Data|Crime]]. |name = SetPCEnemyOfFaction |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = faction |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = enemyFlag |Type = int }} |example = SetPCEnemyOfFaction BigTownFaction 1 }} ==See Also== *[[GetPCEnemyOfFaction]] *[[ClearFactionPlayerEnemyFlag]] *[[ClearActorsFactionsPlayerEnemyFlag]] *[[GetActorCrimePlayerEnemy]] *[[GetActorFactionPlayerEnemy]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Crime Functions]] [[Category:Faction Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Player Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Expels the player from the specified faction, and sets the "expelled" flag to true (see [[GetPCExpelled]]). If the player is in a faction-owned cell, he will be moved outside through the nearest door.
When iFlag is 0, clears the "expelled" flag. |name = SetPCExpelled |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = FactionID |Type = editorID }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = iFlag |Type = bool |Value = 0/1 }} |example = SetPCExpelled MagesGuild 1 }} ==See Also== *[[GetPCExpelled]]
[[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Faction Functions]] [[Category:Player Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Manually forces the player to sleep the specified number of hours. |name = SetPCSleepHours |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = iHours |Type = int }} }} ==See Also== *[[GetPCSleepHours]] *[[ShowSleepWaitMenu]]
[[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Player Functions]] [[Category:Time Functions]] [[Category:UI Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Makes the player use the "toddler walk" animation set. |name = SetPCToddler |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = isToddler |Type = bool |Value = 0/1 }} |example = SetPCToddler 1 }} ==See Also== *[[SetPCYoung]]
[[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Player Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Flags the player as young. A young player only uses the default hair for his race and never loads face attachments. |name = SetPCYoung |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = isYoung |Type = bool |Value = 0/1 }} |example = SetPCYoung 1 }} ==See Also== *[[AgeRace]] *[[MatchRace]]
[[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Player Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Sets the duration of the actor's current package to iDuration (in minutes of gametime). This is useful for situations where you want a package to run for less than an hour. |name = SetPackDuration |referenceType = [actor:ref] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = duration |Type = int }} |example = SetPackDuration 2.0 }}
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:AI Functions]] [[Category:Package Functions]] [[Category:Time Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Sets a specified skill to be the tag skill in a specified index (0-3). Setting a tag skill gives the player a starting bonus in that skill ([[fAVDTagSkillBonus]]). |name = SetPlayerTagSkill |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = SkillName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = iSkillIndex |Type = int |Value = 0-3 }} |example = SetPlayerTagSkill "SmallGuns" 0 Sets the Small Guns skill to be tagged in tag index 0. }} ==Notes== * The game allows up to four tagged skills. Calling this function with an index higher than 3 seems to have no effect, and if more skills are tagged with [[SetTagSkills]] or [[AddTagSkills]], only the first four choices will take effect. ==See Also== *[[IsPlayerTagSkill]]
[[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Player Functions]] [[Category:Actor Value Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Changes the actor's behavior to simulate being a combat teammate of the player. The actor will act and react as if he is in the player faction for the purposes of combat. Other actors will treat the actor as is he were in the player faction. The teammate will imitate the player's sneak state. An actor in the teammate state can be activated in combat. |name = SetPlayerTeammate |referenceType = [actor:ref] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = teammateFlag |Type = int |Value = 0/1 }} |example = BobREF.SetPlayerTeammate 1 }} ==Notes== * Increments the player teammate count. * Teammate state is cleared on death of the actor and player teammate count is decremented. * Changing cells will teleport the teammate to the player * Enables OpenTeammateContainer even with the parameter = 1 * Enables the Companion Wheel (''New Vegas'') * Displays a map marker in the Pip-Boy for the teammate (''New Vegas'') * Essential flagged teammates are not essential in Hardcore (''New Vegas'')
==See Also== *[[GetPlayerTeammate]] *[[GetPlayerTeammateCount]] *[[GetTeammates]] *[[OpenTeammateContainer]] *[[ToggleTeammateKillable]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category: Functions]] [[Category: Miscellaneous Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Follower Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Sets the position of the calling object relative to the world on the specified axis (X, Y, or Z). |name = SetPos |referenceType = [Object] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Axis |Type = char |Value = X, Y, Z }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Pos |Type = float }} |example = ActorRef.SetPos Z 10520 Player.SetPos X 566.34 }} == Notes == *Consider using [[MoveTo]] if you are trying to place objects in remote cells. *This function will work on [[Activator]]s with no issues, but [[Static]], [[Static Collection]]s, [[Tree]], [[Flora]], and [[Furniture]] forms will not be affected unless they are spawned dynamically, likely done via [[PlaceAtMe]]. *Moving an object with '''SetPos''' that supports dynamic Navmesh cutting will not have the cutout update. Use [[Update3D]] after moving to resume normal behavior.
==See Also== *[[SetPosEx]] - a faster version that works on all three axes at once. Also ignores formtype limitations. *[[GetAngle]] *[[GetHeadingAngle]] *[[GetPos]] *[[MoveToCell]] *[[MoveTo]] *[[Rotate]] *[[SetAngle]] *[[ClampToGround]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Movement Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = [[SetQuestDelay]] modifies the processing time delay for a script. The change is save-baked.
By default, quest scripts process every 5 seconds (this is based on an INI setting). This is to prevent the quest scripts from taking up an excessive amount of processor time. Unless you have a good reason, you should leave the quest script processing every 5 seconds.
Good reasons include: *The quest script is running a timer which requires precise timing (e.g. for dialogue). *The quest script needs to react to something with precise timing (e.g. to trigger different parts of a scene).
Even when you have a good reason, avoid setting the quest delay time to .1, or any other low number, for the duration of a quest. Under almost all circumstances, set the quest delay to a low number for a specific purpose, and when that purpose is finished, set it back to the default. |name = SetQuestDelay |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = quest |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = questDelay |Type = float }} |example = SetQuestDelay MyQuest .1 Scripts in MyQuest will process every .1 seconds. }} ==Notes== * Setting delay to 0 tells the quest to process at the INI setting default (5 seconds).
==See Also== *[[GetQuestDelay]] *[[GetSecondsPassed]] *[[GetDelayElapsed]] *[[SetDelayElapsed]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Quest Functions]] [[Category:Script Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Sets the "quest item" flag on an object or actor (1 or 0). When this flag is set to 1: * NPC/Creature -- has priority in updates when the player is changing process state. For instance, "quest" flagged NPCs will follow the player through load doors more quickly. Corpses will not be "cleaned up" when the cell expires. * Inventory items -- player cannot drop or sell items flagged as "quest items". |name = SetQuestObject |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = object |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = questObjectFlag |Type = int |Value = 0/1 }} |example = SetQuestObject Jauffre 1 }} ==Form Types== These form types can be Quest items: 
22: Talking Activator
24: Armor
25: Book
31: Misc
40: Weapon
41: Ammo
42: NPC
43: Creature
46: Key
47: Ingestible
103: Weapon Mod
==See Also== *[[IsQuestItem]] *[[SetQuestItem]] *[[SetShowQuestItems]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Object Functions]] [[Category:Quest Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Set the actor to a restrained state (or clear the state). Restrained actors will not move from their current position, or enter combat, but will continue to "think" (pick packages, run detection so they could yell alarms) and go into dialogue. |name = SetRestrained |referenceType = [actor:ref] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = restrainedFlag |Type = int |Value = 0/1 }} }} ==Notes== *Also prevents the usage of weapons from [[Use Weapon Package]] and [[UseWeapon (Function)]]. The actor will still attempt to run the package and perform various animations but will never attack.
==See Also== *[[GetRestrained]] *[[SetActorsAI]] *[[SetCombatDisabled]] *[[SetUnconscious]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:AI Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Sets the mass of an object, which determines how much it moves when it is bumped into or hit with a weapon. Valid values are 0-100, 100 being no movement. |referenceType = RefID |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = RigidBodyMass |Type = float }} |example = PistolRef.SetRigidBodyMass 100
PistolRef will not be moved at all if bumped into or hit with a weapon }}
==See Also== *[[GetRigidBodyMass]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Object Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Sets the rumble on the Xbox 360 or PlayStation 3 controller. |name = SetRumble |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = leftMotorStrength |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = rightMotorStrength |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = duration |Type = float }} |example = SetRumble 0.5 1 10 The controller will rumble for 10 seconds with a stronger impact on the right hand. }} ==See Also== *[[:Category:Controller Functions (JIP)]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Functions (Consoles)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Opens the CharGen menu to the Attributes page for distributing SPECIAL points. It sets the total amount of SPECIAL points the player has, adding or subtracting points until the given total is reached. |name = SetSPECIALPoints |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = iTotalPoints |Type = int }} |example = SetSPECIALPoints 40 }} ==Notes== *The value given as iTotalPoints represents the '''total number''' of SPECIAL points the player will '''have''' after leaving the menu. *Assuming the player initially has 35 SPECIAL points ( 5 in all 7 Attributes ), calling <tt>SetSPECIALPoints 40</tt> will allow the player to add (40 - 35) = 5 more SPECIAL points to his character, bringing their total to 40. Calling <tt>SetSPECIALPoints 30</tt> will force the player to subtract (35 - 30) = 5 SPECIAL points from his character. *Same functionality as the now-defunct [[ShowChargenMenuParams]] function. ==See Also== *[[ShowSPECIALBookMenuParams]] *[[ShowLoveTesterMenuParams]] *[[AddSPECIALPoints]] *[[SetTagSkills]] *[[AddTagSkills]]
[[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Player Functions]] [[Category:Actor Value Functions]] [[Category:UI Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Sets the scale of the calling object to the specified amount: 1.0 being "normal size", 2.0 being twice normal size, etc. This change is savebaked. |name = SetScale |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = scale |Type = float }} |example = BrahminRef.SetScale 0.9 Sets the scale of BrahminRef to 90% of that which it used to be. }} ==Notes== * SetScale can be used to pseudo-disable items without preventing OnLoad block from loading by using 0 as its parameter * When called on dead actors (tested from console), the value returned by GetScale will update but the size of the actor will stay the same. [[ResurrectActor]] will cause the size of the actor to update, as will calling [[Disable]] followed by [[Enable]]. * The "weight" of Actors will update with their scale, i.e. actors at scale 0.5 will be easier to carry than actors at scale 2.0. * Scale also affects movement speed and damage. ([https://www.nexusmods.com/newvegas/mods/66347 lStewieAl's Tweaks] has options to disable this). * SetScale doesn't always give immediate visual change and often requires leaving the cell the object is in and returning or loading a save, use [[Update3D]] after to ensure scale updates immediately.
==See Also== *[[GetScale]] *[[GetBaseScale]] *[[ModScale]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Scale Functions]] [[Category:Savebaked Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = This function is used to simplify processing during complex scenes involving many actors in combat. As such it needs to be used carefully and only under controlled circumstances, as it significantly changes the behavior of the game while it is set.
'''Important note:''' this flag is cleared every frame, so in order to maintain the "complex scene" flag you need to set it in a script which is running on something in the loaded area (such as a load door or activator).
The important changes the "complex scene" flag make are:
* animation and sound details are reduced * target disposition checks are not run as a part of "should attack" checks for actors already in combat * combat does not check movement restrictions (actors may sidestep into objects, off ledges, etc) * '''low and middle-low process levels do not update''' -- very important! This means that actors which are not in the loaded area WILL NOT UPDATE while this flag is set -- meaning they will not go through load doors, for example. This is the main reason this command needs to be used with caution. * excess dead bodies in the loaded area are cleaned up more frequently * avoidance is disabled (actors won't open doors or angle away from each other) |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = enable |Type = int }} |example = SetSceneIsComplex 1 }}
==See Also== *[[SetAllReachable]] *[[SetAllVisible]] *[[SetNoAvoidance]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category: Functions]] [[Category: AI Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = This is used to show or hide quest items in the player's inventory (normally the player cannot drop or sell quest items). When flag = 1, quest items will be displayed in the player's inventory (default functionality). When flag = 0, quest items will be hidden.
Obviously this function has to be used in very controlled circumstances to prevent the player from permanently "losing" all his quest items. |name = SetShowQuestItems |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = showQuestItemsFlag |Type = int |Value = 0/1 }} }} ==See Also== *[[GetShowQuestItems]] *[[IsQuestItem]] *[[SetQuestObject]] *[[SetQuestItem]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Object Functions]] [[Category:Quest Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Shrink/Grow an actor. Sets the actor size to specified radius (0.0 resets the actor to its original radius). |name = SetSize |alias = CSize |returnType = int |referenceType = [actor:ref] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = size |Type = float }} |example = 
player.SetSize 10.0
player.SetSize 0.0
}} ==Notes== * Sets the radius of the actor's collision bounding box. * May not work correctly on actors other than the player, or at all [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Sets the specified quest stage to "Completed", and resolves any of the attached stage items that pass their conditions (runs results, adds to quest log, completes the quest if it should). |name = SetStage |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = quest |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = stageIndex |Type = int }} |example = SetStage MS27 30 }} ==Notes== * If the quest is not currently running, SetStage will automatically start it. * If this is the first stage of the quest to be added to the Pip-Boy, the player will receive a "Quest Added" message on screen. * SetStage has no effect if called with a StageIndex that doesn't exist in the specified quest. * When called as part of the quest activated by the "New Game" menu option (CG00 in ''Fallout 3'', VCG00 in ''Fallout: New Vegas'') SetStage automatically exits the player from the Main Menu, transitioning them from [[MenuMode]] to [[GameMode]]. * The stage result script will be processed immediately after the SetStage call. The main script containing the SetStage call will be halted until the result script is finished. It is even possible to use the SetStage command inside a stage result script. In this case the new stage result script will run in place of the SetStage command and the old stage result script will continue after the new one has finished. Unlike [[Activate]], it seems that any number of recursive SetStages are allowed (at least 250, tested from the same quest, are possible).
==See Also== *[[GetStage]] *[[GetStageDone]] *[[SetStageAlt]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Quest Functions]] [[Category:Savebaked Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Opens the CharGen menu to the Skills page for selecting Tagged Skills. It sets the total number of tagged skills the player can choose. |name = SetTagSkills |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = addedPoints |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = showInitialTaggedSkills |Type = bool |Optional = y }} |example = SetTagSkills 3 1 }}
==Notes== *When adding points, the number reflects the total number to be selected. Assuming the player initially has 3 Tagged Skills, calling <tt>SetTagSkills 4</tt> will let the player choose (4 - 3) = 1 more Tag Skill. *The optional parameter defaults to 1 (true). If set to 0, any Tag Skills the player initially has are not shown, allowing them to be overwritten. * The game allows up to four tagged skills. If more skills are tagged with this function or [[AddTagSkills]], only the first four choices will take effect.
==See Also== *[[ShowChargenMenuParams]] *[[SetSPECIALPoints]] *[[AddSPECIALPoints]] *[[AddTagSkills]] *[[SetPlayerTagSkill]] *[[IsPlayerTagSkill]]
[[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Player Functions]] [[Category:Actor Value Functions]] [[Category:UI Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Sets the actor whose dialogue should be used for a talking activator object. It is called on a talking activator reference (a computer) and an optional parameter which is the actor who is "talking" through the activator. If no actor is passed in then the talking activator reverts its normal talking behavior. |name = SetTalkingActivatorActor |referenceType = [activator:ref] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = actor |Type = ref |Optional = y }} |example = ComputerRef.SetTalkingActivatorActor BuddyRef Now the computer will talk as if Buddy is speaking through it. ComputerRef.SetTalkingActivatorActor Will set the computer to again talk with the "voice" and dialogue of the computer. }} ==Notes== Talking Activators set via script will have no distance attenuation in the ''New Vegas'' engine. ==See Also== *[[IsActorTalkingThroughActivator]] *[[IsTalkingActivatorActor]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]]

{{Function |CSWikiPage = SetUnconscious |origin = GECK1 |summary = Turns an actor's conscious behavior on or off. Actors that are unconscious will not use AI behavior and will not play idle sounds. When an unconscious actor is activated, a message stating that the actor is unconscious will appear. |referenceType = Actor |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = UnconsciousFlag |Type = int}} |example = Protectron01REF.SetUnconscious 1 Protectron01REF will be set to be unconscious }}
==Notes== *Using this on dead actors will cause them to be resurrected, but will not cause them to play a "get up" animation. As with [[ResurrectActor]], this can cause various animation problems if not called in the same frame as the actor's death.
==See Also== *[[GetKnockedState]] *[[GetUnconscious]] *[[ResurrectActor]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = [[SetVatsTarget]] turns VATS targeting on or off for a destructible object, overriding the object's base setting. |referenceType = DestructibleObject |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = EnableTargeting |Type = int }} |example = TargetMarker01REF.SetVatsTarget 1 }} ==See Also== *[[SetVATSTargetable]] *[[GetVATSTargets]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:VATS Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Sets the current condition (health) of an actor's currently equipped weapon. |name = SetWeaponHealthPerc |referenceType = [actor:ref] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = healthCondition |Type = float }} |example = BruceRef.SetWeaponHealthPerc 50 Sets the condition of Bruce's currently equipped weapon to 50%. }} ==Notes== * In ''Fallout: New Vegas'', this command is hardcoded not to increase condition beyond 99.83%. This is a workaround for a bug concerning odd results for calculations with the condition at a full 100%.
==See Also== *[[GetWeaponHealthPerc]] *[[ModWeaponHealthPerc]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Object Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Changes weather using the games normal switching to the specified weather. The transition time is effected by the Trans Delta setting of the weather or if [[SetWeatherTransitionTimeOverride]] has been used to extend the vanilla maximum of 30 seconds. If OverrideWeatherFlag is 1, the weather will not change again until [[ReleaseWeatherOverride]] is called. Otherwise, the weather will change back in the normal fashion according to the weather system. |name = SetWeather |alias = sw |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = WeatherID |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = WeatherOverrideFlag |Type = bool |Optional = y }} |example = SetWeather MegatonCloudy01 Sets the current weather to MegatonCloudy01, without overriding the weather change. SetWeather WastelandClear 1 Sets the current weather to WastelandClear, but overrides so no other weather can happen until [[ReleaseWeatherOverride]] is called. ref rCurrentWeather set rCurrentWeather to WastelandClear sw rCurrentWeather Stores WastelandClear in a Reference before it is set ref rCurrentWeather set rCurrentWeather to GetCurrentWeather SetWeatherTransitionTimeOverride 90 sw rCurrentWeather Sets the current weather to [[GetCurrentWeather]] so it doesn't require explicit REF IDs and uses [[SetWeatherTransitionTimeOverride]] to extend the transition period to 90 seconds }} ==See Also== *[[ReleaseWeatherOverride]] Releases the override flag and allows the games climate to switch weather as normal *[[ForceWeather]] Switches weather immediately, but it's a jarring change if the player sees it *[[SetWeatherTransitionTimeOverride]] Allows much longer weather transition periods, far above the vanilla Trans Delta maximum of 30 seconds *[[GetCurrentWeather]] Returns the current weather
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Weather Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Turns respawn on or off on the specified [[Encounter Zone]].
This change is savebaked. |name = SetZoneRespawns |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = encounterZone |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = respawnFlag |Type = int |Value = 0/1 }} |example = SetZoneRespawns YaoGauiDen01 0 Creatures will no longer respawn in the Yao Gaui Den zone. }} ==Notes== * Respawn rate on zones is controlled by a gamesetting: [[iHoursToRespawnCell]] (default setting is 72 hours) * The easiest way to test respawning is using [[SetGameSetting]] in console to change the value and wait outside the zone
==See Also== *[[GetZoneRespawns]] *[[Encounter Zone]] *[[SetContainerRespawns]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Savebaked Functions]] [[Category:Encounter_Zone_Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Changes the gender of the specified actor. The function has an optional parameter indicating the target sex (male, female). If no parameter is passed the actor changes to their opposite gender. If no [[calling reference]] is specified, the player is chosen by default.
If ''resetAppearance'' is set to true (1) AND if the affected actor is the player AND if ''targetSex'' is set to change the actor's current gender (rather than keep the same one), then the player's appearance will be set to the default preset for their race+gender. This default preset can be seen when opening [[ShowRaceMenu]] and changing the race. |name = SexChange |referenceType = [actor:reference] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = targetSex |Type = Male/Female |Optional = y }} {{FunctionArgument |Name = resetAppearance |Type = bool{Default:0} |Optional = y }} }} ==Notes== * If an NPC's chosen hair is now invalid for the changed sex, the default hair is used instead.
==See Also== *[[GetIsSex]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Adds or removes all map markers from the world map. Optional arguments specify whether to enable fast travel and whether to skip markers flagged as hidden. |name = ShowAllMapMarkers |alias = TMM |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = showMarkers |Type = bool }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = canTravel |Type = bool |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = skipHidden |Type = bool |Optional = y }} |example = ShowAllMapMarkers 1 Adds all map markers to the world map and enables fast travel. ''Markers that are flagged as hidden are skipped.'' ShowAllMapMarkers 1, 1, 1 Default values if optional arguments are not specified. Same as first example. ShowAllMapMarkers 0, 0, 0 Removes all map markers from the world map, ''including markers flagged as hidden.'' }}
==Notes== *Markers flagged as hidden have the "Show All" Hidden flag enabled in the reference dialog. *Hiding markers will turn off travel for them regardless of canTravel. *Showing markers with canTravel set to 0 will not disable fast travel for markers that are already fast travel enabled. *If used while the Pipboy is open, it does not update the map until the pipboy is put down and opened back up.
==See Also== *[[GetMapMarkerVisible]] *[[ShowMap]] *[[Reference#Map_Marker|Map Marker Data]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Map Functions]]

revision.comment: The note about 100 discount being free is false, at least for New Vegas
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Displays the Barter Menu. It is called on a Reference and can take 1 optional parameter. The parameter is in the range [ -100, 100 ] and denotes the percentage of discount on Sell prices / inflation on Buy prices. See examples below. |name = ShowBarterMenu |alias = SBM |returnType = void |referenceType = [actor:ref] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = discount |Type = int |Optional = y }} |example = ShowBarterMenu 50 The Barter Menu shows up, and the vendor sells items at 50% of their normal sell price and will buy items at 150% of their normal buy price. }} ==Notes== * The parameter default is 0 (no change to prices).
==See Also== *[[Creating a new vendor]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:UI Functions]]

{{Function |origin = VEGAS1 |summary = Shows the caravan menu. |name = ShowCaravanMenu ''CaravanDeckName'' ''Difficulty'' ''MaxBetPercent'' |example = Jack.ShowCaravanMenu CaravanDeckJack 0 .3}} This will initiate a game of caravan with Jack, with Jack using CaravanDeckJack, playing with a difficulty rating 0 out of 3, and betting at maximum 30% of the currency he holds.
PrimmJohnsonNashRef.ShowCaravanMenu CaravanDeckNash 1 .25 This will initiate a game of caravan with Johnson, with Johnson using CaravanDeckNash, playing with a difficulty rating of 1 out of 3, and betting at maximum 25% of the currency he holds.
==Notes== *The max currency amount for both the player and the opponent include the base value of all '''Faction Currency''' they have.
[[Category:Functions (Vegas)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:UI Functions]]

{{Function |origin = VEGAS1 |summary = Brings up the ''New Vegas'' Vigor Tester menu to allow the player to distribute or redistribute SPECIAL points. |name = ShowLoveTesterMenuParams |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = iPoints |Type = int }} |example = ShowLoveTesterMenuParams 40 }}
==Notes== * iPoints is the total number of SPECIAL points the player will have to distribute.
==See Also== *[[ShowSPECIALBookMenuParams]] *[[SetSPECIALPoints]]
[[Category:Functions (Vegas)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Player Functions]] [[Category:Actor Value Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = [[ShowMessage]] is used to display [[message|messages]] to the player. The message itself is an editor object that contains all the data: display text, type of message, and buttons (if the message is a message box). |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = MessageID |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = var1 |Type = var }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = var2 |Type = var }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = ... }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = var9 |Type = var }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Duration |Type = int |Optional = true }} The number of ''var'' parameters required is equal to the number of [[ShowMessage#Formatting_messages|format specifiers]] in the specified message's content, up to a maximum of 9. |example = ShowMessage MyMessage Shows ''MyMessage''. ShowMessage TimeScaleMessage TimeScale Shows ''TimeScaleMessage'', and inserts the value stored in ''TimeScale'' into it. In order for this to work, ''TimeScaleMessage'' must have the appropriate [[ShowMessage#Formatting_messages|format specifier]] to display a floating point value. }}
==Formatting messages== Variable parameters can be used in conjunction with a Message in order to insert variables into the displayed text. * Use the formatting notation below in the text of the Message. * The number of parameters called by ShowMessage must match the number of variables the message is expecting.
====Formatting notation====
'''%.2f''' - This means format the variable with 2 decimal places.
'''%.0f''' - This will format the variable with 0 decimal places, so is the normal choice for integers.
'''%5.0f''' - The number in front of the point specifies the minimum width of the number. In this case, there will always be enough space in front of the number for 5 digits: Number 12 wins Number 1234 wins
====Formatting switches====
The following formatting switches can be used in Messages. Put them in any sequence right after the '%' {|{{Wikitable}} |- ! Switch ! Function |- |<nowiki>+</nowiki> |Display + in front of positive numbers |- |<nowiki><Space></nowiki> |Leave a leading space in front of positive numbers |- |<nowiki>-</nowiki> |Use left-aligned formation instead of right alligned. |- |<nowiki>0</nowiki> |The filling-char used for formatting is '0' instead of ' ' |}
====Other Functions====
'''%g''' - This usually works just like "%.0f", displaying 0 decimal places. When the number is 1000000 or larger, though, the game diplays it in scientific notation (1E+006)
'''%.3e''' - Shows numbers in scientific notation (123000 = 1.23E+005)
'''%%''' - Use this to display a percent-sign in the message
'''[[sUActn|&sUActn...]]''' - Allows you to insert the key used for a given control, such as the Activate button, the Always Run button, or the Block button. See the '''[[sUActn]]''' article for the list of control codes.
====FOSE/NVSE Switches====
Since this is a vanilla function, it cannot take advantage of the extra FOSE/NVSE [[String Formatting]] functionality.
==Notes== *Buttons are part of the Message form. If no buttons are specified on a MessageBox type message, a default "OK" button will be displayed. *Message Type on the Message determines whether we are displaying a "message" (upper right corner, single line of text, doesn't interrupt the game) or a "message box" (center of the screen, game stops until the player presses one of the buttons). *The optional parameters must be declared variables or globals. You cannot use constant values. *Variables can be inserted into the displayed text (see below). The message will fail to display if the wrong number of variables are passed into the ShowMessage call. *Variables cannot be inserted into a message box's buttons. *If you make messages with multiple choices and want to script changes based on the button pressed then use [[GetButtonPressed]] to check the button pressed. *The "Duration" parameter doesn't actually do anything *It's not recommended to call this command in response to a button choice with [[GetButtonPressed]] from another message already on the screen inside [[MenuMode]] blocks as it can lead to the message not showing when needed, instead use [[GameMode]] if you have multi layered use of [[ShowMessage]].
==See Also== *[[Adding an Options Menu]] *[[GetButtonPressed]] *[[MessageEx]] *[[MessageExAlt]] *[[MessageBoxEx]] *[[MessageBoxExAlt]] *[[ShowTutorialMenu]] is almost identical. *[[ShowWarning]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category: Functions]] [[Category: Miscellaneous Functions]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Message Functions]]

{{Deprecated}}
{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = No parameters. Brings up the "Gene Projector" (''Fallout 3'') or "Reflectron" (''Fallout: New Vegas'') menu which allows the player to select sex, race, face, and hair. |name = ShowRaceMenu |returnType = void |example = ShowRaceMenu }} ==Notes== *If the player changes race in this menu, their Ability [[Actor Effect]]s and temporary Actor Effects are removed. This is likely a leftover from ''Oblivion'', where races had their own innate abilities.
==See Also== *[[TTW_ShowGeneProjector]] *[[ShowBarberMenu]] *[[ShowPlasticSurgeonMenu]]
[[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Player Functions]] [[Category:UI Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Opens the repair menu so that the player may repair their equipment by paying caps. The repair skill of the calling actor will be used, and any caps spent will be added to that actor's inventory. |alias = srm |referenceType = Actor |example = player.ShowRepairMenu Opens the repair menu so that the player's repair skill is used, and any caps spent will be added to the player's inventory. }} ==Notes== *[[ShowRepairMenu]] can be called without a specified reference (for example, via a quest script or the console) without causing a compiler error, but nothing will happen. *If [[ShowRepairMenu]] is called on the player, any caps spent will be added back to the player's inventory so that none are lost, but they must have enough caps in their inventory before they can repair an item.
[[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Player Functions]] [[Category:UI Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Brings up the SPECIAL book menu to allow the player to distribute or redistribute SPECIAL points. |name = ShowSPECIALBookMenuParams |alias = ssbmp |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = iPoints |Type = int }} |example = ShowSPECIALBookMenuParams 40 }} ==Notes== * iPoints is the total number of SPECIAL points the player will have to distribute.
==See Also== *[[ShowLoveTesterMenuParams]] *[[SetSPECIALPoints]]
[[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Player Functions]] [[Category:Actor Value Functions]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Book Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Displays the wait/sleep menu. Has two parameters, the second is optional. The first parameter specifies a wait or a sleep menu, 0 = Wait Menu, 1 = Sleep Menu. The second parameter specifies whether sleep wait preconditions should be checked - default is false. |name = ShowSleepWaitMenu |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = isSleep |Type = int}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = shouldCheckPreconditions |Type = int |Optional = y}} }} ==Example== Display the sleep menu: 
ShowSleepWaitMenu 1 1
==Notes== *If the ''shouldCheckPreconditions'' arg is set to 1, the ''isSleep'' arg is ignored and it will always open the Sleep menu (if the preconditions pass). **This is fixed in [[ShowOff]] version 1.80. ==See Also== *[[MenuMode]] *[[Con_CloseAllMenus]] *[[TapControl]] *[[SHowManyWait]], [[sSleep]] and [[sWait]] gamesettings affect the text shown by this function. *[[GetPCSleepHours]] / [[SetPCSleepHours]] *[[IsTimePassing]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:UI Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |name = ShowTutorialMenu |summary = Shows the specified [[Message]] Box form, similar to [[ShowMessage]], but with slightly different visuals. These are the "VDSG Manual" styled message boxes. |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = MessageID |Type = baseform }} |example = ShowTutorialMenu HelpHealingLimbs }} ==See Also== *[[ShowMessage]] *[[ShowWarning]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Message Functions]]

{{Incomplete}} {{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = This function will output a variety of debug info about the calling reference to the console. |name = ShowViewerStrings |alias = SVS |referenceType = ref |example = player.svs 
--- NiObject ---
this = 2DBE1720
m_uiRefCount = 1
--- NiObjectNET ---
m_kName = Bip01
--- NiAVObject ---
m_bAppCulled = false
m_localTranslate = (0,0,0)
m_localRotate = ((1,0,0),(0,1,0),(0,0,1))
m_fLocalScale = 1
m_worldTranslate = (26229.1,40185.2,14236)
m_worldRotate = ((0.733661,-0.679515,0),(0.679515,0.733661,0),(0,0,1))
m_worldScale = 1
WorldBound = (26248.2, 40171.9, 14263.8) , 62.7746
SelUpdate = true
SelUpdateTransforms = true
SelUpdateRigid = false
SelUpdatePropControllers = false
--- NiNode ---
m_bVisual = true
}} [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |name = ShowWarning |summary = Allows a script to display/write a warning.
This gives the ability to print a message to the warnings.txt file, which is useful for debugging and tracing script flow. |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = message |Type = string }} |example = ShowWarning "This is the warning" }} ==Notes== * This will also throw a message box on the screen unless the user has selected "Yes to All" on a previous warning.
==See Also== * [[ShowMessage]] * [[ShowTutorialMenu]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Debugging]] [[Category:Designer Debug Tools]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Message Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = When given degrees returns the sine of a given angle. |name = Sin |returnVal = sine |returnType = float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = degrees |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = arc |Type = bool |Optional = y }} |example = sin x Will return the sine of x degrees sin x 1 Will return the arcsine of x in degrees. Note the optional 1 indicating arc-. }} ==See Also== *[[Abs]] *[[Cos]] *[[Log]] *[[Pow]] *[[Tan]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Math Functions]]

revision.comment: replaced old beth wiki link to uesp because beth STILL has not brought theirs back
revision.text:
{{Incomplete}}
Link to UESP Construction Set Wiki: [https://cs.uesp.net/wiki/SkipAnim SkipAnim].
This function appears to be broken for ''Fallout 3'' and ''New Vegas''. In testing it causes all actors it is called upon to become permanently frozen, even after the SkipAnim ends.
==See Also== *[[LoopGroup]] *[[PlayGroup]] *[[PlayIdle]] *[[AnimGroups]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Animation Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Will return the square root of x. |name = Sqrt |returnType = float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = value |Type = float }} }} ==Notes== Sin, cos, tan, pow, log, sqrt and abs (particularly pow and log) are extremely slow. Do not use them in script blocks that get executed every frame. Try [[Fsqrt]] instead.
==See Also== *[[Sin]] *[[Cos]] *[[Tan]] *[[Log]] *[[Pow]] *[[Fsqrt]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Math Functions]]

revision.comment: A bunch of testing was done
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Forces the actor to start combat with the specified ActorID. If the target actor is already in combat, the function will cause the attacker to target their combat group rather than that actor specifically. If the target is not immediatly visible to the calling actor [[reference]], the calling actor will slowly make their way towards the targets position in a search like behavior. |name = StartCombat |referenceType = [actor:ref] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = targetActor |Type = ref }} |example = StartCombat player }}
==Notes== *StartCombat will not work if the calling actor [[reference]] is in any [[faction]] that makes them an Ally or Friend of the target actor. *Despite what the function name may indicate, this command does not always immediatly put the calling reference into a combat state unless the target is immediatly visible to the calling reference. Otherwise they will search for the target. *StartCombat will abort the search behavior if the target is too far away or evades detection for too long. When aborted the actor will go back to being non hostile to the target (Assuming the calling reference was not hostile to begin with).
==See Also== *[[IsInCombat]] *[[SendAssaultAlarm]] *[[StopCombat]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Combat Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Forces a conversation between two [[NPC]]s, optionally starting with a specified topic. The calling actor will seek out the target actor in order to have a conversation, no matter where the two NPCs are currently. This can also be used to have an NPC "force greet" the player, by setting the player as the TargetActor. The specified topic will be used instead of the normal Greeting topic, so the NPC can start the dialogue with any topic (use Greeting to simply have the normal greeting). |name = StartConversation |referenceType = sourceActor |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = targetActor |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = topicID |Type = baseform |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = speakerLocationRef |Type = ref |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = targetLocationRef |Type = ref |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = headTrack |Type = int |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = allowMovement |Type = int |Optional = y }} |example = 
SunnyRef.StartConversation Joe SecretTopic
SunnyRef.StartConversation player ForceGreetTopic
SunnyRef.StartConversation player ForceGreetTopic ref1 ref2 1 0
}} ==Optional Parameters== * [speaker location ref] - The Editor Reference ID for the speaker pathing, see notes below. * [target location ref] - The Editor Reference ID for the target pathing, see notes below. * [head track] - Enter 1 to enable head tracking during conversation. Enter 0 to disable. * [allow movement] - Enter 1 to enable movement during conversation.
