Enter 0 to disable. ==Notes== * If called on an NPC who is in combat with the player, the NPC won't start conversation with the chosen topic but with the GREETING topic. * Using the optional 'location refs' for speaker and target presumably facilitates AI pathing if movement is required to start the conversation, which appears especially useful for cell transitions. * If either the ''sourceActor'' or the ''targetActor'' are [[Disable|Disabled]], this function does nothing. ==See Also== *[[Say]] *[[SayTo]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Dialogue Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]]

revision.comment: It's not much, but it's something.

revision.text:
{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Starts the quest, which means: * Any attached dialogue will be enabled. * The quest script, if any, will begin running. |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = questname |Type = EditorID }} }} ==Examples== StartQuest SQ09 Starts the quest with the EditorID of SQ09
==Notes== * Avoid calling this function to start a quest which is already running. If there is a script attached to the quest, any variables will be reset to 0 when the saved game is reloaded.
==See Also== *[[StopQuest]] *[[GetQuestRunning]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Quest Functions]] [[Category:Savebaked Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = StartRadioConversation will trigger a conversation over the specified radio station. The topicID parameter is optional. If a parameter is given, the conversation will begin with the specified topic. If no topic is specified, the station will play a topic according to normal conversation rules. |name = StartRadioConversation |referenceType = [radiostation:ref] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = topicID |Type = baseform |Optional = y }} }} ==Notes== * If the station is currently broadcasting, interrupt the broadcast with the new conversation, then return to generating looping broadcasts as before; if the station is NOT currently broadcasting, just play this single conversation and then stop broadcasting. (i.e. StartRadioConversation does not change the "broadcast state" of the station).
==See Also== *[[GetBroadcastState]] *[[StartConversation]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Radio Stations]] [[Category:Radio Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Ends the combat state for an actor, making them stop fighting. |name = StopCombat |referenceType = [actor:ref] |example = ActorRef.StopCombat ActorRef will stop fighting all nearby targets. }} ==Notes== * If the function is called on an actor with a low disposition or remains aggressive towards nearby actors, they may resume fighting and return to combat state. This function proves most useful for scripted actors entering combat with [[StartCombat]]. * If you wish to stop all enemies from fighting a single actor, refer to [[StopCombatAlarmOnActor]].
==See Also== *[[StopCombatAlarmOnActor]] *[[SetCombatDisabled]] *[[StartCombat]] *[[ToggleAI]] *[[ToggleCombatAI]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:AI Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]]

revision.comment: Add alias (used in StealthBoyStopCombatScript FNV script)
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Ends the fighting flag for all enemies targeting the specified actor. |name = StopCombatAlarmOnActor |alias = SCAOnActor |referenceType = [actor:ref] |example = PlayerRef.StopCombatAlarmOnActor All hostile actors that are in combat with the player will return to their normal routines. }} ==Notes== * This function forcefully ends the fighting flag for all hostile actors/creatures to the specified actor. * If you wish to only stop the fighting flag for a single actor, refer to [[StopCombat]].
==See Also== *[[StopCombat]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:AI Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Cancels forced head-tracking from the [[Look]] function. Actor resumes normal head-tracking behavior. |name = StopLook |returnVal = 0, 1 |returnType = void |referenceType = [Actor] |example = AndyStahlRef.StopLook }} ==See Also== *[[Look]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:AI Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Stops playing the specified magic effect visual. |name = StopMagicEffectVisuals |alias = SME |referenceType = [Reference] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = magicEffect |Type = baseform }} |example = 
StopMagicEffectVisuals SABS
sme SHLD
}} ==See Also== *[[PlayMagicEffectVisuals]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category: Functions]] [[Category:Game Effect Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Stops playing the specified [[Effect Shader]]. |name = StopMagicShaderVisuals |alias = SMS |referenceType = [actor:ref] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = effectShader |Type = baseform }} |example = 
BuddyRef.StopMagicShaderVisuals Smoke01
BuddyRef.sms Smoke01
}} ==Notes== * There is no need to stop an effect shader that you called with a duration. * If a Particle Shader utilizes ''Full Particle Birth Count'', then this function will not forcefully stop the particles from emitting. This function will only stop a Particle Shader if it uses ''Persistent Particle Count''. ==See Also== * [[PlayMagicShaderVisuals]] * [[HasEffectShader]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Game Effect Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Stops the quest, which means: * Any attached dialogue will be disabled. * The quest script, if any, will stop running after its current iteration (see [[StopQuest#Notes|note below]]). |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = questname |Type = EditorID }} }} ==Examples== StopQuest SQ09 Stops the quest with the [[EditorID]] of SQ09
==Notes== * This function does not act as a return function when called from a quest's own script. The script will continue running until the end of the last block.
==See Also== *[[StartQuest]] *[[GetQuestRunning]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Quest Functions]] [[Category:Savebaked Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Tells an actor to resume normal package behavior after being put into a "wait" state by the [[Wait]] command. |name = StopWaiting |referenceType = [Actor] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = PackageID }} |example = 
BuddyRef.StopWaiting FollowPlayerPackage
Buddy will stop waiting after previously being told to wait using [[Wait]] }} ==See Also== *[[IsWaiting]] *[[Wait]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:AI Functions]]

{{Function |origin = VEGAS1 |summary = Used to swap a texture for a particular nif block on a given reference. |name = SwapTextureOnRef ''Ref'' ''NifBlockName'' ''PathToNewTexture'' |alias = SwapTexture ''Ref'' ''NifBlockName'' ''PathToNewTexture'' |returnType = void |example = 
SwapTexture Player "##Screen:0" "weapons\1handpistol\EuclidScreenSuccess"
Swaps the texture for the nif block named ##Screen:0 on the player to EuclidScreenSuccess. 
string_var sFace
string_var sExpression
SwapTextureOnRef SecuritronRef "Screen01:0" ("creatures\securitron\" + $sFace + "_" + $sExpression)
Swaps the face+expression texture for the target Securitron reference. Works like [[SetSecuritronExpression]], except this isn't [[savebaked]]; the effect is undone whenever the reference is unloaded. To keep the effect active, one may have to scan the cells for actor references and re-apply the effects upon noticing that a specific Securitron reference is now loaded. }} ==Notes== * Do not include "Data\Textures" or "Textures\" at the start of the path to the new texture. Also be sure to '''not''' include the file extension at the end of the path. Using an invalid texture or an invalid path will result in either nothing happening, or that particular nif block turning pink. * NifBlockName DOESN'T have to contain "##" at the start, unless the name of that particular block actually contains "##" within the model itself. * Loading a save reverts all altered textures back to their initial state. In order to prevent this from happening, you might need to use NVSE's [[GetGameLoaded]]. ==See also== * [[SwapTextureEx]] [[Category:Functions (Vegas)]] [[Category:Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Creates a special save labeled SYS, which does not appear in the Save menu (only Load), and of which there can only be one copy. If the function is used again, the new save will overwrite the previous one. |name = SystemSave |returnType = void |example = SystemSave }}
==Notes== *The base game only uses the function right before the player can initiate the battle for Hoover Dam.
==See Also== *[[Autosave]] *[[ForceSave]] *[[SaveGame]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = When given degrees returns the tangent of a given angle. |name = Tan |returnVal = tan |returnType = float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = degrees |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = arc |Type = bool |Optional = y }} |example = tan x Will return the tangent of x degrees tan x 1 Will return the arctangent of x in degrees. Note the optional 1 indicating arc-. }} ==See Also== *[[Abs]] *[[Cos]] *[[Log]] *[[Pow]] *[[Sin]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Math Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Triggers shudder effect on HUD. There are four optional parameters for intensity and duration.. |name = TriggerHUDShudder |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = val1 |Type = float |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = val2 |Type = float |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = val3 |Type = float |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = val4 |Type = float |Optional = y }} |example = TriggerHUDShudder 2 2 }} ==See Also== *[[TriggerHitShader]] *[[SetCameraShake]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Used to manually trigger the "hit shader" effect of blurring the screen (normally used when the player is staggered). Commonly used in traps, other "earth shaking" activators, and inebriation effects.
The strength parameter can go up as high as you want, but anything higher than 4 or 5 starts to lose the desired effect. |name = TriggerHitShader |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = strength |Type = float }} }} ==See Also== *[[TriggerHUDShudder]] *[[SetCameraShake]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Shows the blood effect on screen which will slowly fade away. Higher the number the more blood you'll see, maximum is 100. |name = TriggerScreenBlood |alias = TSB |returnType = traitValue:float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = count |Type = int |Value = 1-100 }} |example = TriggerScreenBlood 10 }}
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = UnequipItem forces an actor to unequip an item. |name = UnequipItem |returnType = void |referenceType = [ActorRefID] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ObjectID |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = NoEquipFlag |Type = int |Value = 0, 1 |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = HideEquipMessage |Type = int |Value = 0, 1 |Optional = y }} |example = Player.UnequipItem HuntingRifle 0 1 }} ==Parameters== *If NoEquipFlag = 1, actor (including the player) will be unable to equip the item. Once the item is equipped with the [[EquipItem]] function, the item can be equipped and unequipped as normal. *If HideEquipMessage = 1, the item unequipped message will be hidden from the player.
==Notes== * This function '''does not trigger the [[OnUnequip]] script event''' (where appropriate). Use [[UnequipItemAlt]] if that functionality is desired. * The ''NoEquipItem'' flag is effectively ignored by Companions. Calling "UnequipItem <itemname> 1" on a Companion will ''not'' prevent them from re-equipping the item if it is the best equipment available. * If UnequipItem is called on the player while they're viewing their inventory, whether or not the visual "equipped" indicator is updated immediately depends on the HideEquipMessage flag. If it's set to 0, the graphic will update immediately, but if it's set to 1 the graphic won't update until another graphic updates. Running the following code will prevent this bug: UnequipItem ObjectID 0 1 AddItem ObjectID 1 1 RemoveItem ObjectID 1 1 Alternatively, [[RefreshItemsList]] can be used.
==See Also== *[[DropAlt]] *[[UnequipItemAlt]] *[[EquipItem]] *[[EquipItemAlt]] *[[UnequipItems]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Object Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |CSWikiPage = Unlock |origin = GECK1 |summary = The '''Unlock''' script function unlocks terminals and locks. |name = Unlock |returnType = void |referenceType = [ObjectRefID] |example = TerminalRef01.Unlock Unlocks TerminalRef01. }} ==Notes== * Player will have access regardless of lock level or previously being locked out.
==See Also== *[[GetIsLockBroken]] *[[GetLocked]] *[[GetLockLevel]] *[[Lock]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Lock_Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = VEGAS1 |summary = Enables a [[Challenge]] on the player's challenges screen which had been flagged with "Start Disabled", allowing it to progress. |name = UnlockChallenge |returnVal = |returnType = void |referenceType = |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ChallengeID |Type = ref }} |example = UnlockChallenge NVChallengeUsingThat }} ==See Also== *[[ChallengeLocked]] *[[GetChallengeFlags]] can determine if "Start Disabled" has been flagged.
[[Category:Functions (Vegas)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Challenge Functions]] [[Category:Challenge Functions (Vegas)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Has an actor use its weapon. It is called on a Reference and takes a whopping nine parameters, though the last five are optional: * the weapon * where to stand * the target * how many times to use the weapon * whether to crouch when reloading [optional, default is true] * whether to hold fire when blocked [optional, default is true] * whether to automagically hit the target [for scripted forced kills; optional, default is false] * whether to prevent all damage [for scripted protracted fake combat; otpional, default is false] * where the target needs to stand [optional, default/NULL means "anywhere"] |name = UseWeapon |referenceType = actor |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = weapontoUse |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = whereToStand |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = target |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = numberOfShots |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = crouchToReload |Type = int |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = holdFire |Type = int |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = alwaysHit |Type = int |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = doNoDamage |Type = int |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = targetShouldBeHere |Type = int |Optional = y }} |example = BuddyRef.UseWeapon Pistol12 BuddyRef HollyRef 5 0 0 0 0 XMarkerRef047 So Buddy would draw his pistol (or grab one if one was nearby), and from his own current location he would fire at HollyRef five times -- but only while she is near XMarkerRef047. }} ==See Also== *[[Use Weapon Package]] *[[FireWeapon]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:AI Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Tells an actor to go into "wait" behavior for the specified package. Works only if PackageID is the actor's current package, and if the current package is a Follow or Accompany package. |name = Wait |referenceType = [Actor] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = PackageID }} |example = 
BuddyRef.Wait FollowPlayerPackage
Buddy will wait until told otherwise using [[StopWaiting]] }} ==See Also== *[[IsWaiting]] *[[StopWaiting]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:AI Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Manually closes the sleep menu (as if the player had pressed the Cancel button). |name = WakeUpPC |example = WakeUpPC Will interrupt the player's sleeping, as if the player pressed cancel. Will not work if the player is waiting. }} ==See Also== *[[IsPCSleeping]] *[[GetPCSleepHours]] *[[SetPCSleepHours]] *[[TapKey]] *[[Con_CloseAllMenus]]
[[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Player_Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

Functions grouped by type. [[Category:Functions (All)]] [[Category:Scripting]] [[Category:Functions]]

{{Bc|Functions|Functions|Functions (GECK)|Functions (GECK)}} Functions added by the [[Fallout Script Extender]] (for Fallout 3). Most of these functions are available in [[NVSE]]. [[Category:Functions (All)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]]

revision.comment: Added link to JIP Script Runner Tutorial
revision.text:
{{Bc|Functions}} This page lists functions added by the [http://www.nexusmods.com/newvegas/mods/58277 JIP LN NVSE Plugin]
==Features== *[[Adding lights to models in NifSkope]] *[[IsControlPressed]]/[[IsControlDisabled|Disabled]] are made to also work with controller inputs. *[[JIP LN NVSE Script Runner Introduction]] allows scripts to be executed without an esp.
[[Category:Functions (All)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:NVSE]]

{{Bc|Functions}} Functions added by the [[New Vegas Script Extender]]. Note that if the article states "A function added by the New Vegas Script Extender" in the version line at the top, then it is not included in [[Fallout Script Extender|Fallout 3's FOSE]]. However, the [[:Category:Functions (FOSE)|FOSE functions]] are included in NVSE.
==See Also== *[[String Variable]] *[[Array Variable]] *[[User Defined Function]] *[[Debug Dumps]] [[Category:Functions (All)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:NVSE]]

NX, also known as the 'NVSE Extender' is a plugin for NVSE. It adds a number of functions, and a new extended variable type, usually referred to as 'NX variables', which are persistent across game sessions. ==NX Variables== NX variables are stored on any reference, and manipulated via a string key. They may be of type [[float]] (EVFl), [[Ref Variable|form]] (EVFo) or [[String Variable|string]] (EVSt), and a reference may have any number associated with it. Keys for each type must be unique, altough alternative types may share the same key. NX variables are accessible from any loaded mod, and are therefore an excellent way for intermod communication without the need for masters- if a mod references a key that does not exist, the value 0 is simply returned. However, since keys must be unique it is advisable and good compatability etiquette to prefix them with a mod identifier. Keys are case insensitive. 
float fMood

SunnyREF.NX_SetEVFl "MyMod:Mood", 67

let fMood := SunnyREF.NX_GetEVFl "MyMod:Mood" ; * returns 67

ref rPet

SunnyREF.NX_SetEVFo "MyMod:Pet", CheyenneREF

let rPet := SunnyREF.NX_GetEVFo "MyMod:Pet" ; * returns CheyenneREF

string_var role

SunnyREF.NX_SetEVSt "MyMod:Role", "Goodspring's Gecko Hunter"

let role := SunnyREF.NX_GetEVSt "MyMod:Role" ; * returns above
==See Also== *[[Associating data with a reference]] *[[New Vegas Script Extender]] *[[Auxiliary Variables]] - A more modern equivalent added by JIP LN NVSE. ==External Links== *[http://www.loverslab.com/topic/24656-nvse-extender-aka-nx/ Download Page] *[http://www.loverslab.com/topic/22270-fnv-scripting-nx-variables/ A tutorial on NX variables] [[Category:Functions (All)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:NVSE]] [[Category:Variables]]

{{Function | origin = GECK1 | summary = '''GetAnimAction''' is a script function and condition to see what special animation action an actor is currently doing. | name = GetAnimAction | returnVal = actionIndex | returnType = int | referenceType = Actor | example = if BuddyRef.GetAnimAction == 8 Determines if Buddy is performing the reload animation. | CategoryList = [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] }} ==Notes==
Return Values: {| class="wikitable" border="1" ! Index ! Action (revised for GECK FO3) |- | -1 ||none |- |0 ||ANIM_ACTION_EQUIP_WEAPON |- |1 ||ANIM_ACTION_UNEQUIP_WEAPON |- |2 ||ANIM_ACTION_ATTACK |- |3 ||ANIM_ACTION_ATTACK_FOLLOW_THROUGH |- |4 ||ANIM_ACTION_ATTACK_THROW |- |5 ||ANIM_ACTION_ATTACK_THROW_ATTACH |- |6 ||ANIM_ACTION_BLOCK |- |7 ||ANIM_ACTION_RECOIL |- |8 ||ANIM_ACTION_RELOAD |- |9 ||ANIM_ACTION_STAGGER |- |10 ||ANIM_ACTION_DODGE |- |11 ||ANIM_ACTION_WAIT_FOR_LOWER_BODY_ANIM |- |12 ||ANIM_ACTION_WAIT_FOR_SPECIAL_IDLE |- |13 ||ANIM_ACTION_FORCE_SCRIPT_ANIM |} {| class="wikitable" border="1" ! Index ! Action (revised for GECK NV) |- | -1 ||none |- |0 ||ANIM_ACTION_EQUIP_WEAPON |- |1 ||ANIM_ACTION_UNEQUIP_WEAPON |- |2 ||ANIM_ACTION_ATTACK - Firing full auto or doing a melee swing |- |3 ||ANIM_ACTION_ATTACK_FOLLOW_THROUGH - Returned when firing semi auto |- |4 ||ANIM_ACTION_ATTACK_THROW - Returned when returning to the static animation after the attack animation was performed. That includes semi-auto recoil, melee after swing, and drawing another throw-able weapon after one was just thrown. |- |5 ||ANIM_ACTION_ATTACK_THROW_ATTACH - Returned when gearing up to throw |- |6 ||ANIM_ACTION_??? - Returned when the throwable weapon has left the players hand |- |7 ||ANIM_ACTION_BLOCK |- |8 ||ANIM_ACTION_STAGGER - Returned when staggered by an npc that is blocking |- |9 ||ANIM_ACTION_Reload - Returned when reloading a weapon |- |10 ||ANIM_ACTION_DODGE |- |11 ||ANIM_ACTION_WAIT_FOR_LOWER_BODY_ANIM |- |12 ||ANIM_ACTION_WAIT_FOR_SPECIAL_IDLE |- |13 ||ANIM_ACTION_FORCE_SCRIPT_ANIM - Returned for pretty much every action not included in the ones above.This includes furniture anim, playidle anims, staggering from crippled limbs, getting up from the ground after getting knocked down, and so on. |}
=Notes:= *Not all values have been figured out. The NV ones are correct as to the latest info garnered from extensive testing. Values 10-12 may be left overs, 11 is more than likely one for horses(?). ==See Also== *[[SetOnAnimActionEventHandler]] *[[IsAnimPlaying]] / [[IsAnimPlayingEx]] *[[IsIdlePlaying]] / [[IsIdlePlayingEx]] *[[GetCurrentAIProcedure]] *[[GetLastPlayerAction]] *[[IsPlayerActionActive]] [[Category:Animation Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function | origin = GECK1 | summary = Returns whether the actor is currently performing a Sandman action. This relates to the ''Mister Sandman'' perk which allows the player to silently kill NPCs when they're sleeping. | name = GetSandman | returnType = bool | referenceType = Actor | example = player.GetSandman Returns 1 if the player is currently performing a Sandman action. | CategoryList = [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] }} [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function | origin = GECK1 | summary = The victim gets cannibalized by the cannibal, which regains some health. | name = StartCannibal | returnType = void | referenceType = Actor | arg0 = Cannibal | arg0Type = ref | example = VictimRef.StartCannibal player The victim will be cannibilized by the player. }} ==Notes== *Cannibalizing someone is a hostile act. *It's possible to cannibalize living [[:Category:Actors|actors]]. As the cannibalize animation is intended to be used on corpses this could yield strange looking results.
==See Also== *[[HasBeenEaten]] *[[DonnerReedKuruParty]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]]

{{Function |origin = VEGAS1 |summary = Returns the last target of the owner of the calling reference. |name = GetOwnerLastTarget |referenceType = [Form] |example = ref rOwner set rOwner to WeaponForm.GetOwnerLastTarget }} =='''See Also'''== *[[GetCombatTarget]]
[[Category:Functions (Vegas)]] [[Category:Functions]]

{{Function |origin = VEGAS1 |summary = Returns the current reputation with a certain faction. Its two parameters are the reputation ID in question and an integer to denote mixed, good, or bad where 0 = mixed, 1 = good, and 2 = bad. The player is only ever on one of these sliders at a time. If GetReputationThreshold always returns a value between 0 and 6. If the function is called with a faction which the player has both fame and infamy equal to zero, it will return a value of 1 in all three cases of checking the mixed, good, and bad axes. If the player has a higher fame than infamy, GetReputationThreshold will return a number between 2 and 6 on the fame axis depending upon how high the player's fame is, and 0 for both mixed and infamy reputations, and so on.
|returnType = void |referenceType = |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ReputationID |Type = editorID }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Mixed/Good/Bad |Type = int }} }} ==Examples==
if GetReputationThreshold RepNVGoodsprings 1 > 1
Returns 1 if the player is currently on the '''good''' axis with the Goodsprings faction.
if GetReputationThreshold RepNVGoodsprings 0 == 6
Returns 1 if the player has the highest level of '''mixed''' reputation with the Goodsprings faction, i.e. Wild Child.
==Values== Below is a list of values that GetReputationThreshold can output and the meaning for each. GetReputationThreshold ''ReputationID:editorID'' 2 6 = Villified 5 = Hated 4 = Shunned 3 = Merciful Thug 2 = Sneering Punk 1 = Neutral 0 = None of the above
GetReputationThreshold ''ReputationID:editorID'' 1 6 = Idolized 5 = Liked 4 = Accepted 3 = Good Natured Rascal 2 = Smiling Troublemaker 1 = Neutral 0 = None of the above
GetReputationThreshold ''ReputationID:editorID'' 0 5 = Wild Child 4 = Unpredicatable 3 = Mixed 2 = Dark Hero or Soft Hearted Devil 1 = Neutral 0 = None of the above
==See Also== *[[GetReputation]] *[[GetReputationPct]] *[[AddReputation]]
[[Category:Functions (Vegas)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Reputation Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]]

{{Function |origin = VEGAS1 |summary = Returns how far the player is along a reputation's fame or infamy, in the form of a decimal between 0 and 1. The result is the same as if one had divided the player's raw fame or infamy value by the given reputation's maximum value, or '''scale'''.
The second arg determines if infamy (0) of fame (1) is checked. |returnType = void |referenceType = |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = FactionID |Type = editorID }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Fame/Infamy |Type = int }} }} ==Examples==
if GetReputationPct RepNVGoodsprings 1 == 1
True if the player's '''fame''' with the Goodsprings faction is at its maximum.
if GetReputationPct RepNVGoodsprings 0 < 0.5
True if the player's '''infamy''' with the Goodsprings faction is below 50% of its maximum.
==See Also== *[[GetReputation]] *[[GetReputationThreshold]] *[[AddReputation]]
[[Category:Functions (Vegas)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Reputation Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]]

revision.comment: Tweaked order of example line documentation to be more intuitive
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = VEGAS1 |summary = Adds an exact amount of reputation (Fame/Infamy) with a certain faction.
|returnType = void |referenceType = |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = FactionReputationID |Type = baseform |Optional = n }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Infamy/Fame |Type = int |Optional = n }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Amount |Type = float |Optional = n }} }}
'''AddReputationExact''' adds an ''exact'' amount of reputation to a faction, unlike [[AddReputation]], which only allows 1-5 (small deed - big deed).
The first parameter determines if Infamy (0) or Fame (1) should be added, whereas the second parameter determines the exact amount.
=='''Examples'''==
AddReputation RepNVGoodsprings 0 5
Adds 5 '''Infamy''' to the Goodsprings faction.
AddReputation RepNVGoodsprings 1 10
Adds 10 '''Fame''' to the Goodsprings faction.
=='''Notes'''==
*This function can technically be used to remove reputation by having ''Amount'' be a negative number, however, it is better to use [[RemoveReputationExact]] for this purpose because with '''AddReputationExact''' the message will always say "gained" rather than "removed."
=='''See Also'''== *[[GetReputation]] *[[AddReputation]] *[[RemoveReputation]] *[[RemoveReputationExact]]
[[Category:Functions (Vegas)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Reputation Functions]] [[Category:Savebaked Functions]]

{{Function |origin = VEGAS1 |summary = Adds a certain amount of reputation (fame/infamy) with a certain faction.
|returnType = void |referenceType = |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = FactionID |Type = editorID |Optional = n }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Fame/Infamy |Type = int |Optional = n }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Amount |Type = int |Optional = n }} }}
'''RemoveReputation''' removes a certain amount of reputation to a faction, unlike [[RemoveReputationExact]], it only allows 1-5, whereas 1 is a small deed forgotten, and 5 a big deed.
The first integer determines if infamy (0) of fame (1) should be removed, whereas the second integer determines the amount (1-5)
If one wishes to remove an exact amount of reputation, use [[RemoveReputationExact]].
The amount of reputation points that these integers correspond to are determined by game settings.
*(Integer) = (Amount of reputation points actually removed) **1 = [[fReputationBumpVeryMinor]], Default Setting = 1 **2 = [[fReputationBumpMinor]], Default Setting = 2 **3 = [[fReputationBumpAverage]], Default Setting = 4 **4 = [[fReputationBumpMajor]], Default Setting = 7 **5 = [[fReputationBumpVeryMajor]], Default Setting = 12
=='''Examples'''==
RemoveReputation RepNVGoodsprings 0 5
The player has somehow gotten this faction to forget a very major '''bad''' deed against them, causing a big loss of infamy equal to [[fReputationBumpVeryMajor]].
RemoveReputation RepNVGoodsprings 1 1
The player has somehow gotten this faction to forget a very minor '''good''' deed, causing a small loss of fame equal to [[fReputationBumpVeryMinor]].
=='''See Also'''== *[[GetReputation]] *[[AddReputation]] *[[RemoveReputationExact]]
[[Category:Functions (Vegas)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Reputation Functions]]

{{Function |origin = VEGAS1 |summary = Returns the current reputation with a certain faction. Currently, when used in-game with the console or when used at all without an if, it fails to return a value, so it is best used as an if statement like in the examples.
The second arg determines if infamy (0) of fame (1) is checked.
|returnType = void |referenceType = |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = FactionID |Type = editorID |Optional = n }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Fame/Infamy |Type = int |Optional = n }} }} =='''Examples'''==
if GetReputation RepNVGoodsprings 1 > 10
Returns 1 if the player has more than 10 '''fame''' with the Goodsprings faction.
if GetReputation RepNVGoodsprings 0 < 30
Returns 1 if the player has less than 30 '''infamy''' with the Goodsprings faction.
=='''See Also'''==
*[[GetReputationThreshold]] *[[GetReputationPct]] *[[AddReputation]]
[[Category:Functions (Vegas)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Reputation Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]]

{{Function |origin = VEGAS1 |summary = Adds a certain amount of reputation (infamy/fame) with a certain faction.
|returnType = void |referenceType = |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = FactionReputation |Type = baseform |Optional = n }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Infamy/Fame |Type = int |Optional = n }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Amount |Type = int |Optional = n }} }}
'''AddReputation''' adds a certain amount of reputation to a faction, unlike [[AddReputationExact]], it only allows 1-5, whereas 1 is a small deed done, and 5 is a big deed.
The first integer determines if infamy (0) of fame (1) should be added, whereas the second integer determines the exact amount (1-5)
If one wishes to add an exact amount of reputation, use [[AddReputationExact]].
The amount of reputation points that these integers correspond to are determined by game settings.
*(Integer) = (Amount of reputation points actually added) **1 = [[fReputationBumpVeryMinor]], Default Setting = 1 **2 = [[fReputationBumpMinor]], Default Setting = 2 **3 = [[fReputationBumpAverage]], Default Setting = 4 **4 = [[fReputationBumpMajor]], Default Setting = 7 **5 = [[fReputationBumpVeryMajor]], Default Setting = 12
=='''Examples'''==
AddReputation RepNVGoodsprings 0 5
The player has done a big deed ''against'' this faction, causing a big gain of infamy equal to [[fReputationBumpVeryMajor]].
AddReputation RepNVGoodsprings 1 1
The player has done a small deed ''for'' this faction, causing a small gain of fame equal to [[fReputationBumpVeryMinor]].
=='''Notes'''==
*This function can technically be used to remove reputation by having Amount be a negative number, however, it is better to use [[RemoveReputation]] for this purpose because with '''AddReputation''' the message will always say "gained" rather than "removed."
=='''See Also'''== *[[GetReputation]] *[[AddReputationExact]] *[[RemoveReputation]]
[[Category:Functions (Vegas)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Reputation Functions]] [[Category:Savebaked Functions]]

revision.comment: Made it say 'Removes' instead of 'Adds' for what it does to reputation listed at the top.
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = VEGAS1 |summary = Removes an exact amount of reputation (Fame/Infamy) with a certain faction.
|returnType = void |referenceType = |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = FactionID |Type = editorID |Optional = n }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Fame/Infamy |Type = int |Optional = n }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Amount |Type = float |Optional = n }} }}
'''RemoveReputationExact''' removes an ''exact'' amount of reputation to a faction, unlike [[RemoveReputation]], which only allows for preset values (small deed - big deed).
The first parameter determines if Infamy (0) or Fame (1) should be removed, whereas the second parameter determines the exact amount.
=='''Examples'''==
RemoveReputationExact RepNVGoodsprings 0 0.5
Removes 0.5 '''Infamy''' from the Goodsprings faction.
RemoveReputationExact RepNVGoodsprings 1 10
Removes 10 '''Fame''' from the Goodsprings faction.
=='''Notes'''==
*[[RemoveReputation]] and '''RemoveReputationExact''' are only used by default in cases where the player is granted forgiveness. It should not be used, for example, as a way to punish the player for bad deeds by "removing" positive reputation. For that, add infamy using the function [[AddReputation]].
=='''See Also'''== *[[GetReputation]] *[[AddReputationExact]] *[[RemoveReputation]]
[[Category:Functions (Vegas)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Reputation Functions]]

{{Function |origin = VEGAS1 |summary = '''EnableLoadingMenu''' forces a loading screen to appear for a few seconds. This loading screen does not use any loading screens specific to the current location. |name = EnableLoadingMenu 1 }}
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Loading Screen Functions]]

{{Function |origin = VEGAS1 |summary = Returns the player's current winnings level for the specified casino form. Used as a condition function in dialogue for the casino's npcs. |name = GetCasinoWinningStage |returnVal = 0-4 |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = casino |Type = baseform }} }} ==See Also== *[[GetCasinoWinningsLevel]] [[Category:Functions (Vegas)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Casino Functions]]

{{Function |origin = VEGAS1 |summary = Returns the player's current winnings level for the specified casino form. Used as a script function in the casino form's associated script to handle giving the Achievement as well as a dialogue package to an employee to tell the player they've been banned.
[[GetCasinoWinningStage]] does the same, but as a condition function. |name = GetCasinoWinningsLevel |returnVal = 0-4 |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = casino |Type = baseform }} |example = if GetCasinoWinningsLevel TheTopsCasinoData == 1 }} ==See Also== *[[GetCasinoWinningStage]] [[Category:Functions (Vegas)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Casino Functions]]

{{Function |origin = VEGAS1 |summary = Returns 1 if a [[Challenge]] has been completed. If the challenge is set to "Recurring", will return 1 if it was completed at least once. |returnVal = 0, 1 |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Challenge |Type = form }} |example = GetChallengeCompleted MyChallenge }} [[Category:Functions (Vegas)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Challenge Functions (Vegas)]] [[Category:Challenge Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns the actor's [[GetAV|current]] Fatigue [[Actor Value]] divided by their [[GetBaseAV|base]] Fatigue. |name = GetFatiguePercentage |alias = GetFatigue |returnType = float |referenceType = actorREFR |example = 
float fPerc
let fPerc := RaulRef.GetFatiguePercentage
}} ==See Also== *[[GetHealthPercentage]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Actor Value Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]
{{Bc|Functions}} A list of function aliases. [[Category:Functions (All)]] [[Category:Functions]]

{{Incomplete}} {{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns true if calling actor has line of sight to the specified object. Can only be called on actors. |name = GetLineOfSight |alias = GetLOS |returnType = bool |referenceType = [actor:ref] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = target |Type = ref }} |example = testActor.GetLineOfSight TestMarker }} ==Notes== * When called on a non-player actor, the supplied ObjectID must be another actor or getLOS will return false, even when the object is within line of sight. * When called on the player... ** ...non-actors can be specified as an objectID. *** If the objectID parameter is not an actor, however, this function is quite slow and should not be run every frame. ** ...it returns true if the ''camera'' can see the object, '''not''' if the player-character can see the object. This means that in Third-Person, GetLOS may return true for objects ''behind'' the player, if they are close enough that they can be seen with the camera. * When called on a disabled actor, it crashes the game. May CTD if GetLOS is called by an actor that was disabled in the previous frame. A delay of a few frames after the actor is enabled will solve this issue. * The field of view for LOS is +/- 95 degrees from the 0 axis line in which the observer (non-player actor, player, or camera) is looking straight ahead. ** All creatures and non-player actors have the same field of view for LOS, regardless of eye placement, or if they even have heads (zombies) or eyes (will-o-wisps). *Will return true with trigger volumes and collision volumes if they are in the calling actors field of view even though they normally aren't visible in game. *Will not return true when checking if a disabled [[reference]] is in view.
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = VEGAS1 |summary = Sets the [[Actor]] to always show subtitles. |returnType = void |referenceType = ActorREF |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Toggle |Type = int |Value = 0,1 }} |example = SunnyREF.AlwaysShowActorSubtitles 1 }} ==Notes== *This function is cleared every game load.
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:UI Functions]]

{{Function |origin = VEGAS1 |summary = A condition function that returns true if a challenge has "Start disabled" flag. |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = challenge |Type = baseform }} |example = set iLocked to ChallengeLocked NVSomeChallenge }} ==See Also== *[[UnlockChallenge]] *[[GetChallengeFlags]]
[[Category:Functions (Vegas)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Challenge Functions (Vegas)]] [[Category:Challenge Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Detonates all C4 placed by the specified actor. |name = DetonatePlacedExplosives |alias = DPE |returnType = void |referenceType = [actor:ref] |example = Player.DetonatePlacedExplosives }}
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Projectile Functions]]

{{Function |origin = VEGAS1 |summary = Turns Hardcore mode on or off |name = SetHardcore |referenceType = [Actor] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = setORremove |Type = int |Value = 1/0 }} |example = ref.SetHardcore 1 }}
==Notes== *This function seems to do nothing when called with the player as the reference. Using the function in an [[OnTrigger]] script block is probably best, with the direct or implicit reference the trigger object itself.
==See Also== *[[DisableHardcoreTracking]] *[[IsHardcore]]
[[Category:Functions (Vegas)]] [[Category:Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns whether the [[SetInChargen]] state is set. |name = GetInCharGen |returnType = inCharGen:1/0 |example = set bInCharGen to GetInCharGen }} ==Notes== * This function is not available as a condition on actor effects. ==See Also== * [[SetInChargen]] * [[IsInCharGen]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Condition_Functions]]

#REDIRECT [[Special Variables]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Time Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns 1 if the actor is currently in a force sneak state. |name = GetForceSneak |returnType = success:0/1 |referenceType = actor |example = if (actorRef.GetForceSneak == 1) ; in forced sneak state endif }}
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Actor State Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Resets Experience Points (XP) of the player to zero. It is not called on a reference. |name = ResetXP |example = ResetXP Player XP is reset to zero. }} ==Notes== * This function is used in the character revision script that pops up as you leave Goodsprings in ''New Vegas''.
==See Also== *[[RewardXP]]
[[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Player Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns 1 if actor is ignoring friendly hits. |name = GetIgnoreFriendlyHits |returnVal = ignoringFriendlyHits |returnType = int |example = BobREF.GetIgnoreFriendlyHits Returns 1 if actor is ignoring friendly hits. }} ==See Also== *[[SetIgnoreFriendlyHits]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category: Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:AI Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]] [[Category:Condition Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns true if the specified ref is in the given cell. Same functionality as GetInCell, except that this function does not run on a reference -- the actor is supplied as a parameter. |name = GetInCellParam |returnVal = 0, 1 |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Cell |Type = EditorID }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Actor |Type = RefID }} |example = 
GetInCellParam Megaton InternJohnRef
Returns true if Intern John is currently is any Megaton cell.. }} ==Notes== *The CellName has to be a valid interior cell name. Exterior cells will not work. In some cases, it is necessary to create a "dummy" interior cell (e.g. Megaton) in order to use GetInCell for a set of related interior and exterior cells (e.g. all the MegatonXXX cells). ==See Also== *[[GetInSameCell]] *[[GetInWorldspace]] *[[GetInGrid]] (Useful for exteriors) *[[GetInGridInCell]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Actor State Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Cell Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = VEGAS1 |summary = Sets the essential state of a specific actor without touching the base, useful when there are multiple actors with the same base loaded in at once (Ghost People for example). This change is saved with the game. |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Flag |Type = boolean }} |example = ActorREF.SetActorRefEssential 1 Sets the referenced actor to essential. }} ==Notes== *SetActorRefEssential '''cannot''' set an actor to unessential if the actor's base is set essential through the GECK or by [[SetEssential]].
==See Also== *[[SetEssential]] *[[IsEssential]]
[[Category:Functions (Vegas)]] [[Category:Functions]]
{{Bc|Functions}} This page lists functions added by the [http://www.nexusmods.com/newvegas/mods/66927 JohnnyGuitar NVSE] plugin. [[Category:Functions (All)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:NVSE]]

{{Function |origin = VEGAS1 |summary = Adds a playing card to the player's Caravan deck. |name = AddCardToPlayer |alias = AddCard |returnType = void |referenceType = CardRef |example = CardClubs10bREF.AddCardToPlayer }}
[[Category:Functions (Vegas)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Player Functions]] [[Category:Savebaked Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |name = GetTalkedToPCParam |summary = Returns 1 if the NPC has ever spoken to the player, 0 otherwise. Functionally identical to [[GetTalkedToPC]] but an NPC is passed as an argument instead of using a calling reference. |returnVal = 0, 1 |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Actor |Type = ref }} |example = GetTalkedToPCParam BuddyREF Will return a 1 if Buddy has ever talked to the player, and 0 otherwise. }}
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Actor State Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns 1 if player's class matches a given Class ID. |returnType = boolean |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Class |Type = form }} |example = if GetPCIsClass Doctor ; Do my doctor stuff endif }} ==See Also== *[[GetIsClass]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Actor State Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns weight class of a calling actor's equipped armor. Return values are as follows:
0: None
1: Light armor
2: Medium or Heavy armor
|returnVal = 0, 1, 2 |returnType = int |referenceType = Actor |example = Player.GetArmorRatingUpperBody }} ==See Also== *[[GetArmorRating]] *[[GetArmorClass]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Armor Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Opens calling [[Terminal]] reference with hacking minigame. |returnType = void |referenceType = [TerminalREF] |example = TerminalREF.ShowHackingMiniGame ==Notes== *If calling reference is not a Terminal (or missing) the game will crash.
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Terminal Functions]] }}

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Displays interface of a calling [[Terminal]] reference. If a Terminal is locked, hacking minigame will be bypassed. |returnType = void |referenceType = [TerminalREF] |example = TerminalREF.ShowComputersInterface ==Notes== *If calling reference is not a Terminal (or missing) the game will crash.
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Terminal Functions]] }}

{{Function |origin = VEGAS1 |summary = Used to set a Securitron [[reference]]'s expression on their monitor. Requires a reference and two strings. |name = SetSecuritronExpression |alias = SetSecExp |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Target |Type = reference }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Face |Type = String }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Expression |Type = String }} |example = SetSecuritronExpression Lucky38UpgradeSecuritron03 Infantry Alert SetSecExp VMQTopsSecMessengerREF Victor Neutral }}
==Notes== * This function persists across saves and game restarts. * This function works based off of the filename of the textures located in the Textures\Creatures\Securitron\ folder. If one wishes to use this function, any custom textures must be added to the aforementioned directory, and they must be named in a similar fashion to the vanilla textures, e.g. CustomTron_Sad.dds. This could then be called via script like so: SetSecuritronExpression SecuritronRef CustomTron Sad
==See Also== *[[SwapTextureOnRef]] - can be used as a non-persistent alternative to this function, see example.
[[Category:Functions (Vegas)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Texture Functions]] [[Category:Savebaked Functions]]

{{Function |origin = VEGAS1 |summary = Used to open the recipe menu from an [[Actor]] or [[Activator]]. |name = ShowRecipeMenu |alias = Craft |referenceType = [Actor, Activator] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = RecipeCategory |Type = Form }} |example = EDE1REF.Craft AmmoSubRecipes }}
[[Category:Functions (Vegas)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Recipe Functions]]
{{Bc|Functions}} This page contains all functions available in the GECK upon release. [[Category:Functions (All)]] [[Category:Functions]]

{{Function |origin = VEGAS1 |summary = '''IncrementScriptedChallenge''' is a function used by the ''Dead Money'' add-on for ''Fallout: New Vegas''. It increments the progress towards reaching the threshold of the challenge. |name = IncrementScriptedChallenge |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Challenge |Type = form }} |example = IncrementScriptedChallenge MyChallenge }}
[[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Functions (Vegas)]] [[Category:Challenge Functions]] [[Category:Challenge Functions (Vegas)]]

{{Bc|Functions|Functions|Functions (GECK)|Functions (GECK)}}
This page contains all functions added in the New Vegas GECK. See [[:Category:Functions (NVSE)]] for functions available in the Fallout New Vegas Script Extender. [[Category:Functions (All)]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]]

{{Bc|Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)}} This page lists event handling functions added by the [http://www.nexusmods.com/newvegas/mods/66927 JohnnyGuitar NVSE Plugin].
==See Also== *[[Event Handling]] *[[:Category:Event Handlers (JIP)|JIP Event Handlers]] [[Category:Functions (All)]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Event Handlers]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Checks if an item that matches a [[Form Type IDs | Form Type]] is currently being used or activated, returning 1 if it does. The full name of the Form Type must be passed, '''NOT''' its numeric ID. |returnVal = 0/1 |returnType = bool |referenceType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = FormType |Type = string }} |example = GetIsUsedItemType Activator Returns 1 if an activator is being used. }} ==See Also== *[[GetIsUsedItemEquipType]] *[[GetIsUsedItem]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Object Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = VEGAS1 |summary = Displays Slot Machine menu. [[Player]] must meet Casino requirements for the menu to display, such as having Casino Chips in the inventory and not being banned from the casino. |alias = sslmp |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Casino |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = minBet |Type = int |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = maxBet |Type = int |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = maxWinnings |Type = int |Optional = y }} |example = ShowSlotMachineMenuParams TheTopsCasinoData 1 50 500 }} [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Functions (Vegas)]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Casino Functions]]

{{Function |origin = VEGAS1 |summary = Displays Roulette menu. Player must meet Casino requirements for the menu to display, such as having Casino Chips in the inventory and not being banned from the casino. |alias = srmp |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Casino |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = minBet |Type = int |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = maxBet |Type = int |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = maxWinnings |Type = int |Optional = y }} |example = ShowRouletteMenuParams TheTopsCasinoData 1 50 500 }} [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Functions (Vegas)]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Casino Functions]]

{{Function |origin = VEGAS1 |summary = Displays the [[MenuMode|BlackJack menu]]. Player must meet Casino requirements for the menu to display, such as having Casino Chips in the inventory and not being banned from the casino. |alias = sbjmp |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Casino |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = minBet |Type = int |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = maxBet |Type = int |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = maxWinnings |Type = int |Optional = y }} |example = ShowBlackJackMenuParams TheTopsCasinoData 1 50 500 }} [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Functions (Vegas)]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Casino Functions]]

{{Function |origin = VEGAS1 |summary = Returns 1 if hardcore mode has always been activated in the game. This is used to check if player fails requirements before adding achievement for hardcore mode. |name = IsAlwaysHardcore |alias = behemoth |returnVal = 0, 1 |returnType = int |referenceType = [Actor] |example = set bAlwaysHardcore to PlayerRef.IsAlwaysHardcore }} ==See Also== [[IsHardcore]] [[Category:Functions (Vegas)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]]

{{Bc|Functions}}
An xNVSE plugin that allows you to edit and save scripts in GECK and update them live into the game without needing to restart it.
It also allows scripts to be edited and saved with any Text Editor (with the GECK still open). A nice utility to have for any mod maker of Fallout New Vegas.
Download here: [https://www.nexusmods.com/newvegas/mods/70962?tab=description nexusmods.com]
[[Category:Functions (All)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:NVSE]] [[Category:Mod Tools]]

{{Function |origin = VEGAS1 |summary = A condition function that returns 1 if the actor is currently in the specified region. |name = PlayerInRegion |returnVal = 0, 1 |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Type = RegionID }} |example = PlayerInRegion VMapGoodspringsRegion }}
== Notes == *If you're in a sub-region of a larger region, it will return 1 for both.
==See Also== *[[GetInWorldspace]] *[[GetInCell]] *[[IsPlayerInRegion]] - cannot be used as a condition, but can be used in scripts.
[[Category:Functions (Vegas)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Player Functions]]

{{Bc|Functions}} This page lists functions added by the [https://www.nexusmods.com/newvegas/mods/73160 SUP NVSE Plugin]
[[Category:Functions (All)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:NVSE]]

{{Function |origin = BookMenu |summary = Activates the [[Book]] [[calling reference]] manually, opening the restored book menu for the given Book. Optionally allows the "Take" button to be hidden. |name = ShowBookMenu |referenceType = bookReference |Optional = optional |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = showTakeButton |Type = 0/1 |Optional = optional }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = book |Type = baseform |Optional = optional }} }} ==Notes== * This function behaves similarly to doing 'bookref.activate player', with the exception of having an optional argument which allows the "Take" button to be hidden. * This ordinarily unused in ''Fallout 3''/''Fallout: New Vegas'', but can be restored with [[:Category:Functions (BookMenu)|Book Menu Restored]].
==See Also== *[[Activate]]
[[Category:Functions (BookMenu)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Book Functions]] [[Category:UI Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns 1 if the calling actor belongs to at least one evil faction and no non-evil factions. |name = IsActorEvil |returnType = bool |referenceType = [actor:ref] |example = if BuddyRef.IsActorEvil ;do evil stuff }} ==Notes== * This function only works on NPCs, not creatures. No creatures are considered evil, regardless of which factions they belong to.
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category: Functions]] [[Category:Faction Functions]] [[Category: Condition Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Bc|Functions}}
kNVSE, or kNVSE Animation Plugin, is an [[NVSE]] plugin that enables support in the engine for custom weapon animations, custom walking animations and a lot more.
Despite being named similarly to xNVSE, which replaces NVSE, kNVSE is an add-on for xNVSE, and does not replace it.
It is available [https://www.nexusmods.com/newvegas/mods/71336?tab=description here].
==Text keys==
kNVSE supplies numerous custom text keys which can be placed in an animation's KF file or through kNVSE's script functions.
* '''interruptLoop''': AttackLoop will behave more like AttackLoopIS where the animation gets interrupted at the end. Makes for superior automatic weapon animations. It also allows AttackLoop and AttackLoopIS animations to finish before Aim or AimIS animations are played again. * '''burstFire''': Allows multiple “hit” and optionally “eject” keys to activate within a single animation. * '''respectEndKey''': First person animation’s text key won’t be tied to the third person animation text keys. * '''noBlend''': Disables blending into this animation. It is recommended to use this for first person animations but not third person animations. * '''Script''': MyUDFScript: A user defined function script will be called once the time value is reached. In this example the script has the editor ID MyUDFScript * '''blendToReloadLoop''': modifies the behavior of Reload(X/Y/Z/W)Start anim group so that it blends directly to ReloadLoop instead of blending first to Aim, then to - '''ReloadLoop''' which causes a significant pause. Requires both anims to have different priorities. * '''soundPath''': path: Plays sound (.wav file relative to Datafolder) at time specified in value * '''scriptLine''': print "This will print": Runs script line at time specified in value. %r may be used to specify newline for multiline scripts. * '''allowAttack''': Allow the player to attack after this time in reload or equip animations. * '''noFix''': kNVSE on load will try to fix some presumed errors in animation files. If kNVSE is something erroneously to your anim, place this text key in your anim and kNVSE will not try to fix it. * '''allowClampInLocomotion''': By default the game only allows LOOP anims to be registered as locomotion anims. If you need your animation to bypass this, add this text key.
[[Category:Functions (All)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:NVSE]] [[Category:Mod Tools]]

{{Bc|Functions}} This page lists functions added by Tale of Two Wastelands.
[https://taleoftwowastelands.com/ Tale of Two Wastelands] is a large mod that brings the entirety of Fallout 3 into the Fallout New Vegas engine, allowing play of both wastelands, with travel between the wastes. [[Category:Functions (All)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:NVSE]]

{{Bc|Functions}}
The MCM, or Mod Configuration Menu, is a mod that allows configuration of options via the game's main menu for any external mod that supports it, without the requirement for it being a master.
It also includes a plugin for [[NVSE]] to facilitate its features, although if installed, these [[:Category:Functions_(MCM)|functions]] are available to anyone.
The Fallout: New Vegas version is available [http://www.nexusmods.com/newvegas/mods/42507 here]. [[Category:Functions (All)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:NVSE]] [[Category:Mod Tools]]

{{Bc|Functions}} This page lists functions added by [[Book_Menu_Restored_Plugin|Book Menu Restored]] plugin for ([https://www.nexusmods.com/fallout3/mods/23794 Fallout 3]) and ([https://www.nexusmods.com/newvegas/mods/67278 Fallout: New Vegas]). [[Category:Functions (All)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:NVSE]]

{{Bc|Functions}} This page lists functions added by the [https://github.com/Demorome/Showoff-NVSE ShowOff NVSE Plugin].
To get its currently installed version, use `[[GetPluginVersion]] "ShowOffNVSE Plugin"`. *Example return values: **"100" for version 1.0 **"125" for version 1.25

page.title: Complete List of Functions in Fallout 3

revision.text:
The following is a complete list of all registered functions available in ''Fallout 3'' and its [[FOSE]] extenders. Note that some may be [[Console Functions|console only]], and unavailable in the GECK. Some may also be [[Unused Functions|unused and non-functional]], while others may be undocumented.
'''See also:''' *[[:Category:Functions_(All)|Functions (All)]] *[[:Category:Scripting|Scripting]] *[[:Category:Functions|Functions (Category)]] *[[:Category:Function Types|Functions Grouped By Type]]
=== Registered Functions ===
== A == *[[Abs]] *[[Activate]] *[[AddAchievement]] *[[AddCreatureToLeveledList]] *[[AddFlames]] *[[AddFormToFormList]] *[[AddItem]] *[[AddItemHealthPercent]] *[[AddItemToLeveledList]] *[[AddNPCToLeveledList]] *[[AddNote]] *[[AddNoteNS]] *[[AddPerk]] *[[AddSPECIALPoints]] *[[AddScriptPackage]] *[[AddSpell]] *[[AddSpellNS]] *[[AddTagSkills]] *[[AddToFaction]] *[[AddTopic]] *[[AdvancePCLevel]] *[[AdvancePCSkill]] *[[AgeRace]] *[[ApplyImageSpaceModifier]] *[[AttachAshPile]] *[[AutoDisplayObjectives]] *[[Autosave]] *[[AuxiliaryVariableErase]] *[[AuxiliaryVariableEraseAll]] *[[AuxiliaryVariableGetFloat]] *[[AuxiliaryVariableGetRef]] *[[AuxiliaryVariableGetSize]] *[[AuxiliaryVariableGetType]] *[[AuxiliaryVariableSetFloat]] *[[AuxiliaryVariableSetRef]]
== B == *[[BuildRef]]
== C == *[[CanHaveFlames]] *[[Cast]] *[[CastImmediateOnSelf]] *[[Ceil]] *[[ClearActorsFactionsPlayerEnemyFlag]] *[[ClearDestruction]] *[[ClearFactionPlayerEnemyFlag]] *[[ClearNoActivationSound]] *[[ClearOwnership]] *[[ClickMenuButton]] *[[ClickMenuTile]] *[[CloseActiveMenu]] *[[CompareNames]] *[[CompleteAllObjectives]] *[[CompleteQuest]] *[[con_CloseAllMenus]] *[[con_GetINISetting]] *[[con_LoadGame]] *[[con_QuitGame]] *[[con_RefreshINI]] *[[con_Save]] *[[con_SaveINI]] *[[con_SetCameraFOV]] *[[con_SetGameSetting]] *[[con_SetINISetting]] *[[con_SetVel]] *[[Cos]] *[[CreateDetectionEvent]] *[[CreatureHasModel]]
== D == *[[DamageActorValue]] *[[DamageObject]] *[[DebugPrint]] *[[Disable]] *[[DisableAllActors]] *[[DisableAllMines]] *[[DisableButton]] *[[DisableControl]] *[[DisableKey]] *[[DisableLinkedPathPoints]] *[[DisableNavMesh]] *[[DisableNavMeshAlt]] *[[DisablePlayerControls]] *[[DisableTrigger]] *[[Dispel]] *[[DispelAllSpells]] *[[Drop]] *[[DropMe]] *[[DumpMasterFileSeekData]] *[[DuplicateAllItems]] *[[DuplicateNPCStats]]
== E == *[[EditorIDToFormID]] *[[Enable]] *[[EnableButton]] *[[EnableControl]] *[[EnableFastTravel]] *[[EnableKey]] *[[EnableLinkedPathPoints]] *[[EnableNavMesh]] *[[EnableNavMeshAlt]] *[[EnablePlayerControls]] *[[EnableTrigger]] *[[EnterTrigger]] *[[EquipItem]] *[[EquipItemAlt]] *[[EssentialDeathReload]] *[[EvaluatePackage]] *[[EvaluateSpellConditions]] *[[Exists]] *[[ExitGame]] *[[Exp]]
== F == *[[FaceObject]] *[[FadeSFX]] *[[FakeQuantityMenu]] *[[FileExists]] *[[FireWeapon]] *[[Floor]] *[[fmod]] *[[ForceActiveQuest]] *[[ForceActorValue]] *[[ForceFlee]] *[[ForceRadioStationUpdate]] *[[ForceSave]] *[[ForceTakeCover]] *[[ForceTerminalBack]] *[[ForceWeather]] *[[FreePlayer]] *[[FreezeTime]] *[[FriendDispositionBoost]]
== G == *[[GetActionRef]] *[[GetActorAggroRadiusViolated]] *[[GetActorCrimePlayerEnemy]] *[[GetActorFactionPlayerEnemy]] *[[GetActorValue]] *[[GetActorsInHigh]] *[[GetAlarmed]] *[[GetAltControl]] *[[GetAlwaysRun]] *[[GetAmountSoldStolen]] *[[GetAngle]] *[[GetAnimAction]] *[[GetArmorAR]] *[[GetArmorAR]] *[[GetArmorRating]] *[[GetArmorRatingUpperBody]] *[[GetAttackDamage]] *[[GetAttacked]] revision.text:
{{Bc|Functions (ShowOff NVSE)}} This page lists event handling functions and event handlers added by the [[ShowOff NVSE]] plugin.
==See Also== *[[Event Handling]]
[[Category:Functions (All)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Event Handlers]] [[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]]
{{Bc|Functions}} This page lists functions added by [https://www.nexusmods.com/newvegas/mods/74012 AnhNVSE Plugin]. [[Category:Functions (All)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:NVSE]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = ShowOff |originVersion = 1.35 |name = SayTo_GetTopicInfo |summary = Returns the current [[Topic Info]] that the [[calling reference|calling]] [[SayTo]..."
Function is added by NV GECK, not FO3.
revision.text: {{Function |origin = VEGAS1 |summary = Sets the amount of reputation (fame/infamy) with a certain faction, overriding previous value. |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Reputation |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Fame/Infamy |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Amount |Type = float }} |example = SetReputation RepNVGoodsprings 0 5 }} [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions (Vegas)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Reputation Functions]]

{{Function |name = GetSelfAlt |origin = NVSE |originVersion = 6.2.8 |summary = Returns the calling reference, or a null form if there is none. Only useful when this information is accessed externally, or when the scripted reference needs to be passed as a parameter to a function like [[PushActorAway]] Unlike [[GetSelf]], this will always return the calling reference, even if it happens to be a non-Persistent [[Item]]. If that check is not needed, then this is a strictly faster version of that function. |alias = ThisAlt |example = ref rSelf let rSelf := GetSelfAlt player.PushActorAway rSelf 5 }} ==Notes== While functions that take an implied reference can be cast without needing a variable or call to [[GetSelfAlt]], like so: 
CIOS someSpell
Doing so may not be clear, and makes the code less portable if moved to a different script that does not support the implied reference. For this reason, modders should prefer the more expressive syntax: 
ref rSelf
let rSelf := GetSelfAlt
rSelf.CIOS someSpell
Not only for [[CIOS]], but for all "reference methods" that require a reference, like CIOS. ==See Also== *[[GetSelf]] *[[GetContainer]] [[Category:Functions (NVSE)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Reference Variable Functions]] [[Category:ID Functions]]
